attachment.content:
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- spec-human: Create a human-reviewable architecture spec using a 6-section template (Summary, Background, Architecture, Interface, Edge Cases, Alternatives) BEFORE implementing a non-trivial design. Triggered explicitly via /spec-human <topic>, or when the user says "write a human spec", "create a review-ready design doc", "spec this out for review", or "I need a spec for X before we build it". Produces an ASCII markdown document at ./specs/<slug>.md aimed at peer engineers and tech leads who need to validate core architecture and trade-offs in 5 minutes. Do NOT auto-trigger on general design questions or coding tasks - this is a deliberate review gate that only fires on explicit request. For LLM-targeted specs (future sibling skill), use /spec-llm instead.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Review code, tests, and docs for inconsistencies

content.text: I'll review the codebase for inconsistencies and legacy code. Let me first explore the project structure across three dimensions in parallel.

input.prompt:
You are auditing the Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator at /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim for inconsistencies between code and documentation.

Context: The project has a `./hsim` driver (the end-user entry point) with subcommands like `build`, `test`, `run`, `list`, `estimate`. There's also documentation at:
- /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/CLAUDE.md
- /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/docs/USER_GUIDE.md
- /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/driver_ROADMAP.md
- /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/README.md (if exists)

Recent commits show features F11 (run --mode icount/slice), F12 (./hsim estimate), F13 (--mmap-size + HSIM_MMAP_DIR) were added.

Please find inconsistencies between:
1. What `./hsim` actually supports (read the driver script) vs. what docs claim
2. CLI flags / env vars mentioned in docs but missing in code (or vice versa)
3. Commands listed in `./hsim list` outputs vs. actually available
4. Stale references in CLAUDE.md to old paths, removed features, or moved files
5. driver_ROADMAP.md states (todo/in-progress/done) vs actual implementation
6. Things mentioned in USER_GUIDE.md that no longer work or have changed

Also check git status - the user mentioned modified files:
- docs/USER_GUIDE.md (M)
- driver_ROADMAP.md (M)
- hsim (M)
- qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c (M)
- tests/fixtures/Makefile (M)

Look at these modifications to see if they're consistent with each other.

Report findings as a punch list. Be specific - cite file paths and line numbers. Group by severity: HIGH (broken/wrong) vs MEDIUM (stale/outdated) vs LOW (cosmetic). Keep under 600 words.

You are auditing the Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator at /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim for legacy/dead code and inconsistencies in the C/C++/Python source.

Key locations:
- include/hybrid/state_abi.h (shared ABI between QEMU/vsim)
- qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c (QEMU TCG plugin)
- verilator/src/hybrid/*.hpp (handoff_controller, resume_driver, state_drain, qemu_handback, v_regfile_xfer)
- scripts/*.sh (build_qemu, build_vsim, build_simpoint, run_e2e, setup)

Find:
1. Dead code: unreachable branches, unused functions/variables, commented-out blocks left behind
2. TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK markers - grep for these
3. Inconsistencies between state_abi.h struct fields and the code that drains/restores them (does every field actually get written by drain and read by restore?)
4. Magic numbers vs named constants (check for hardcoded GPAs, vector lengths, etc.)
5. Stale debug printf / fprintf(stderr,...) left over
6. Functions or files referenced in code/Makefiles but missing from filesystem
7. CMake/Makefile entries that reference removed sources
8. Tier A vs Tier B CSR restore order in resume_driver.hpp - is the order documented in CLAUDE.md (mstatus.VS, vsetvl, vstart, vxsat/vxrm/vcsr, GPR loop) actually matched in code?
9. Inconsistencies between the QEMU plugin's hybrid_state_v1 layout and the vsim consumer (endian, alignment, missing fields)
10. Any code paths gated by HYBRID_FLAG_* flags that look broken or unused

Note files with uncommitted changes (per git status):
- qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c (M)
- tests/fixtures/Makefile (M)
- verilator (m - submodule)

Look at diffs of those modified files to see what changed and whether they introduce inconsistencies.

Also check the new untracked files: gemm.c, gemm.elf, hello.c, tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c - are these meant to be committed or junk?

Report as a punch list. Be specific - cite file:line. Group HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW severity. Keep under 700 words.

You are auditing test coverage and test-related inconsistencies in the Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator at /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim.

Key locations:
- tests/fixtures/*.c, *.S, Makefile
- tests/fixtures/runtime/ (crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c, rt_c.ld)
- tests/hybrid/e2e.sh (unified harness)
- tests/hybrid/*.test.cpp (doctest unit tests)
- tests/hsim/*.sh (driver tests)
- verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake (CTest registration)
- scripts/run_e2e.sh

Find:
1. Test fixtures listed in Makefile or HybridConfig.cmake that no longer exist (or vice versa - orphan fixtures)
2. Fixtures with their build rules but never registered in CTest
3. Test scripts in tests/hsim/ that reference removed `./hsim` subcommands
4. Inconsistent fixture naming (rt_c_* vs other prefixes), missing entries
5. The new untracked file tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c and tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh - what do they test? Are they registered anywhere?
6. tests/fixtures/Makefile has uncommitted changes (M) - what changed?
7. C-level fixtures that violate the Phase-2 rule (syscalls between enter_vsim/exit_vsim) - the rule from CLAUDE.md: no printf/semihosting between enter and exit
8. Doctest files in tests/hybrid/ that test removed APIs
9. Coverage gaps: any *.hpp in verilator/src/hybrid/ without a corresponding test
10. Documentation in CLAUDE.md about "two fixture families: asm + C-level" - is the current set still accurately described?

Also look at:
- gemm.c and hello.c at the repo root (untracked) - are these stray test artifacts that should be in tests/fixtures/ or deleted?
- gemm.elf (untracked) - definitely a build artifact that shouldn't be committed

Report as a punch list. Be specific - cite file:line. Group HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW severity. Keep under 600 words.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the final audit report:

## Test Coverage & Inconsistency Audit Report

**Scope:** Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator test infrastructure audit

---

### HIGH SEVERITY

1. **test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh unregistered in any test harness** (NEW FILE)
   - File: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh` (untracked)
   - Tests F14 feature: `./hsim estimate` without `--icount` runs to natural exit and drains state
   - **Issue:** No reference in `scripts/run_e2e.sh`, no CTest registration, no mention in HybridConfig.cmake
   - **Impact:** Feature tests won't execute in CI; regressions undetected
   - **Fix:** Register shell test in a CI harness or document as manual-only

2. **no_trigger_exit.c fixture untracked but Makefile pending**
   - File: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c` (untracked)
   - Referenced by: `test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh` (line 22)
   - **Issue:** `tests/fixtures/Makefile` has uncommitted changes (M); `no_trigger_exit` added to `RT_C_FIXTURES` (lines 32–33) but not committed
   - **Fix:** `git add tests/fixtures/Makefile tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c && git commit`

3. **Stray build artifacts at repo root**
   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/gemm.c` (untracked) — test C source
   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hello.c` (untracked, malformed: `int int main(...)`)
   - `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/gemm.elf` (untracked, 56 KB binary)
   - **Issue:** Should either move to `tests/fixtures/` with proper naming or delete
   - **Impact:** Uncommitted artifacts pollute `git status`

---

### MEDIUM SEVERITY

4. **Missing doctest coverage for 4 .hpp files**
   - No test files exist for:
     - `verilator/src/hybrid/dmi_poll.hpp`
     - `verilator/src/hybrid/exit_codes.hpp`
     - `verilator/src/hybrid/gdbstub_session.hpp`
     - `verilator/src/hybrid/systemc_dmi_bus.hpp`
   - **Issue:** Headers define structs/classes but have no registered unit tests in HybridConfig.cmake
   - **Fix:** Either implement `*.test.cpp` files + register in line 102+ of HybridConfig.cmake, or document why no tests exist (e.g., integration-only)

5. **program_buffer.test.cpp exists but unregistered**
   - File: `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/verilator/tests/hybrid/program_buffer.test.cpp` exists
   - **Issue:** Not listed in `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` doctest registration section
   - **Fix:** Add to HybridConfig.cmake (mirror pattern of csr_trigger, icount_trigger, etc.)

6. **Shell test discovery unclear**
   - Tests in `tests/hsim/`, `tests/plugin/`, `tests/drivers/` exist but no global registry
   - `scripts/run_e2e.sh` does NOT call hsim tests; only rounds up `tests/e2e.sh` for round-trip fixtures
   - **Issue:** Individual shell tests in `tests/hsim/test_*.sh` can only be run manually; no CI enforcement
   - **Fix:** Document expected execution pattern (manual vs. CI) or wire into a CTest harness

---

### LOW SEVERITY

7. **CLAUDE.md fixture documentation accurate**
   - Section "two fixture families: asm + C-level" (line 135) correctly describes current state
   - Phase-2 rule (no syscalls between `enter_vsim`/`exit_vsim`) verified in all `rt_c_*.c` fixtures ✓
   - All 7 C-level fixtures properly respect syscall boundary

8. **Fixture naming consistency**
   - All fixtures follow `rt_*` or `rt_c_*` or `probe_v_*` conventions
   - `no_trigger_exit` is the first non-prefixed fixture; consider renaming to `rt_c_no_trigger_exit` for consistency

---

### SUMMARY PUNCH LIST

| Category | Count | Status |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Unregistered test files | 2 | HIGH: `test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh`, `program_buffer.test.cpp` |
| Missing test implementations | 4 | MEDIUM: dmi_poll, exit_codes, gdbstub_session, systemc_dmi_bus |
| Uncommitted changes | 1 | HIGH: `tests/fixtures/Makefile` (pending `no_trigger_exit` fixture) |
| Stray build artifacts | 3 | HIGH: gemm.c, hello.c, gemm.elf at repo root |
| Phase-2 violations | 0 | PASS ✓ |
| Orphan fixtures (defined but missing) | 0 | PASS ✓ |

aiTitle: Review code, tests, and docs for inconsistencies

content.text:
Perfect. Now let me create a final comprehensive report:

## Summary

I've completed a comprehensive audit of the hybrid_sim project for inconsistencies between code and documentation. Here are my findings:

### **HIGH SEVERITY (2 issues)**

**Issue 1: USER_GUIDE.md Cheat Sheet Incomplete** 
- **Location:** `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`, lines 57-63
- **Problem:** The quick-reference table claims `./hsim run <elf> --mode csr|pc [-v]` but the actual code supports **four** modes: `csr|pc|icount|slice`
- **Evidence:** 
  - `hsim` line 1246: `choices=("csr", "pc", "icount", "slice")`
  - Sections 4.3 (M3 icount) and 4.5 (M5 slice) in the same guide document these modes
  - Line 388 shows example: `./hsim run ... --mode icount`
- **Impact:** Users consulting the cheat sheet won't know about icount/slice modes
- **Fix:** Update line 61 to: `./hsim run <elf> --mode csr|pc|icount|slice [-v]`

**Issue 2: CLAUDE.md Example Incomplete**
- **Location:** `CLAUDE.md`, line 24
- **Problem:** Example shows only `csr|pc`: `./hsim run <elf> --mode csr|pc`
- **Same root cause** as Issue 1
- **Fix:** Update to show all four modes available

### **MEDIUM SEVERITY (2 issues)**

**Issue 3: Plugin Parameter drain_at_exit Not Documented**
- **Location:** `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`, Section 2.3 (Plugin args table, lines 315-323)
- **Problem:** Plugin code now supports `drain_at_exit=` parameter (`qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` lines 549-555) but the exhaustive plugin arg table omits it
- **Note:** This is internal to F14; users don't set it directly (hsim does internally), but the table claims to be exhaustive
- **Fix:** Add row: `| drain_at_exit=1 | F14: Run to program exit, count retired insns via plugin callback |`

**Issue 4: ROADMAP Source-of-Truth Path Outdated**
- **Location:** `driver_ROADMAP.md`, line 7
- **Problem:** References `/home/nick/.claude/plans/i-need-to-design-purring-kahan.md` (past session artifact)
- **Impact:** Low—path is informational only, doesn't affect functionality
- **Fix:** Remove or mark as historical reference

### **LOW SEVERITY (cosmetic/informational)**

- **F14 Status Verified:** All features F1–F14 correctly marked `done`
- **HSIM_MMAP_DIR Documentation:** Correctly implemented (Section 2.5, lines 362–402)
- **E2E Baseline:** 45 PASS/0 FAIL assertion in ROADMAP consistent with implementation

### **Verification Results**
- ✓ All 14 features (F1-F14) present and functional
- ✓ All modified files (hsim, USER_GUIDE.md, driver_ROADMAP.md, hybrid_handoff.c, Makefile) consistent with each other
- ✓ No breaking changes to e2e baseline

Perfect. Now let me compile a comprehensive audit report with all findings:

## AUDIT REPORT: Hybrid RISC-V Co-Simulator Code Quality & Consistency

### HIGH SEVERITY

1. **Missing CSR Restoration in Verilator ResumeDriver** `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp:59-125`
   - **Issue**: QEMU plugin drains 11 M-mode CSRs (mepc, mcause, mtval, mscratch, medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, satp, mcycle, minstret) but verilator's `ResumeDriver::resume()` restores only 4 (mstatus, mtvec, mcycle, minstret). The missing 7 CSRs (mepc, mcause, mtval, mscratch, medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, satp) are drained by QEMU1 but silently dropped at the vsim boundary.
   - **Workaround**: PMPs and the missing M-CSRs are restored via QEMU2's gdbstub (`qemu_handback.hpp:266-274, 308-312`), not vsim. This is documented nowhere - the architecture comment implies vsim restores full state.
   - **Severity**: HIGH (architectural asymmetry; silent data loss if vsim execution modifies any trapped CSR before the next gdbstub resync).
   - **Fix**: Document in CLAUDE.md or add assertion that non-mstatus/mtvec/mcycle/minstret CSRs are read-only during phase 2 (vsim).

2. **Inconsistent V-CSR Restore Order Documentation** `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/CLAUDE.md:114-119` vs `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp:88-104`
   - **Issue**: CLAUDE.md lists restore order as `mstatus.VS, vsetvl, vstart, vxsat/vxrm/vcsr, GPR loop`. The code at resume_driver.hpp:88-104 follows this, BUT the comment says mstatus.FS/VS "enables" the extension, yet it also checks `state.mstatus & 0x6000u` (FS field) and `state.mstatus & 0x600u` (VS field) looking for bits that are ALREADY SET - this appears to be checking "is this field non-zero" rather than "is this field enabled". The logic is correct but the comment is confusing.
   - **Fix**: Clarify comment at resume_driver.hpp:72-80 to explain these are checking pre-existing FS/VS bits in the drained mstatus, not enabling them.

3. **Untracked Binary Test Artifacts** `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/gemm.elf`, `gemm.c`, `hello.c`
   - **Issue**: Three test files in root directory appear to be development artifacts, not part of the official test suite. `hello.c` has a syntax error (`int int main`). They clutter the repo and should either be integrated into `tests/fixtures/` or deleted.
   - **Severity**: HIGH (uncommitted junk; `gemm.elf` is 56KB binary in git working tree).
   - **Fix**: Delete or move to `tests/fixtures/` and add to Makefile if needed.

### MEDIUM SEVERITY

4. **F14 Drain-at-Exit Feature Missing from CLAUDE.md** 
   - **Issue**: New drain-at-exit mode (F14, feature flag in `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:108-119`) introduced 3 new globals, 3 new callback functions, and modified state drain logic. Not documented in CLAUDE.md's architecture section. Test harness at `tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh` exists but isn't listed in CLAUDE.md's running tests section.
   - **Severity**: MEDIUM (feature is well-implemented but invisible to new maintainers).
   - **Fix**: Add "F14: Drain-at-Exit Mode" subsection under Trigger Modes in CLAUDE.md, explain the atexit callback chain, and list the new test.

5. **New Fixture `no_trigger_exit` Undocumented in CLAUDE.md**
   - **Issue**: `tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c` (fixture F14) and its build integration (`tests/fixtures/Makefile:32-33`) are committed but not mentioned in CLAUDE.md's Test Fixtures section. The fixture is required for the F14 feature but has no visibility.
   - **Severity**: MEDIUM (fixture is correct but invisible).
   - **Fix**: Add to the C-level fixtures table in CLAUDE.md with note "F14: naturally-exiting fixture with zero `csrwi 0x7C0` insns".

6. **Volatile Qualifier on drained_already May Have Memory Ordering Issues** `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:118`
   - **Issue**: `static volatile int drained_already;` is read in `on_atexit()` (line 352) and written in `on_handoff()` (line 321). The C standard volatile is NOT sufficient for synchronization between signal handlers or atexit callbacks (it's a pragma hint to the compiler, not a memory barrier). If QEMU's atexit callback fires on a different thread than the vCPU that set the flag, there's a race.
   - **Analysis**: In practice, atexit callbacks run in the main thread after all user threads are joined, so this is likely safe. But it's not explicitly guaranteed in the code.
   - **Severity**: MEDIUM (potential race, but unlikely in practice on Unix atexit semantics).
   - **Fix**: Add comment explaining atexit thread safety assumption, or use `std::atomic<int>` if C++ is available.

7. **PMPfg/pmpaddr Not Restored by Verilator Phase** `include/hybrid/state_abi.h:65-66` vs `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` (search yields no results)
   - **Issue**: PMP CSRs are drained by QEMU1 and restored by QEMU2's gdbstub, but Verilator's `StateDrain` and `ResumeDriver` ignore them entirely. If PMP configuration is critical to the workload, silent loss during vsim phase is a problem.
   - **Severity**: MEDIUM (accepted by design since PMP is typically static, but inconsistent with the "full state capture" promise).
   - **Fix**: Document PMP-handling strategy in CLAUDE.md architecture section.

### LOW SEVERITY

8. **Debug fprintf(stderr) Statements Left in Production Code**
   - **qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:311-312**: `fprintf(stderr, "hybrid_handoff: drained state to %s ...` - informational but noisy during parallel test runs.
   - **verilator/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:165**: `std::fprintf(stderr, "exec %s: %s\n", binary.c_str(), strerror(errno));` - error context, probably intentional.
   - **Severity**: LOW (these are debug/error logs, not test garbage).
   - **Fix**: Consider redirecting to a log file in production or gating behind a `--verbose` flag, but acceptable as-is.

9. **stale comment about "unused insn" in CsrTrigger** `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/verilator/src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp:62`
   - **Issue**: Comment says "`insn` is unused (HandoffController decodes the insn only on the RUNTIME arm; PC mode only inserts EXIT)." But `insn` field exists in `CsrTriggerHit` struct and is written at line 51, suggesting it might be used elsewhere.
   - **Severity**: LOW (documentation bug, not a functional issue).
   - **Fix**: Verify if `insn` is actually unused by searching consumers, or clarify the comment.

10. **Missing Endianness Documentation for hybrid_state_v1 Wire Format**
    - **Issue**: `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` and `state_abi_check.h` define struct layout but don't explicitly document endianness (though native x86-64 little-endian is assumed).
    - **Severity**: LOW (would matter if porting to big-endian or cross-compiling, but low risk in current setup).
    - **Fix**: Add comment to state_abi.h line 40: "// All multi-byte fields are little-endian (native on x86-64 / RV64)".

### SUMMARY
- **3 HIGH severity items**: Missing CSR restoration architecture mismatch, untracked binaries, F14 feature undocumented
- **4 MEDIUM severity items**: F14 feature invisible to maintainers, PMP handling design gap, volatile race concern, fixture undocumented  
- **3 LOW severity items**: Debug logs, stale comment, missing endianness doc
- **Code Quality**: Refactor `on_handoff` → `drain_state_to_file` is clean; V-CSR restore order matches docs.
- **New Commits**: Recent drain_at_exit changes (F14) are well-structured and backward-compatible. Test infrastructure solid.

                 pass
 
 
+def _run_drain_at_exit_profile(elf: Path, artifacts: dict[str, Path],
+                                bbv: Path, slice_n: int,
+                                mmap_size: int) -> tuple[int, int]:
+    """F14: run a profile pass that drains at program exit (no icount cap).
+
+    Returns (rc, n_total_insns). On rc != 0, n_total_insns is 0.
+
+    The plugin's drain_at_exit=1 mode counts every retired insn via a
+    per-insn exec cb and overrides the drained state file's `minstret`
+    field with that count (QEMU's PMU minstret is host-tick-derived on
+    system-mode without -icount, which is why we use the plugin counter).
+    """
+    shm_path = _load_hsim_shm().allocate(
+        mmap_size, prefix="hsim-profile-shm.",
+    )
+    state_fd, state_name = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix="hsim-profile-state.")
+    os.close(state_fd)
+    state_out = Path(state_name)
+    try:
+        argv = _qemu_drain_argv(
+            artifacts["qemu"], elf, artifacts["plugin"],
+            shm_path, mmap_size, state_out,
+            plugin_extras=(
+                f"bbv_out={bbv}",
+                f"slice={slice_n}",
+                "drain_at_exit=1",
+            ),
+        )
+        proc = subprocess.run(
+            argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT,
+            stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
+            timeout=_PROFILE_TIMEOUT_S,
+        )
+        if proc.returncode != _QEMU_DRAIN_RC:
+            tail = proc.stdout.decode("utf-8", "replace").splitlines()[-20:]
+            print(f"hsim: QEMU profile expected exit {_QEMU_DRAIN_RC}, "
+                  f"got {proc.returncode}", file=sys.stderr)
+            for line in tail:
+                print(line, file=sys.stderr)
+            return 1, 0
+
+        if not bbv.is_file() or bbv.stat().st_size == 0:
+            print(f"hsim: profile produced empty BBV: {bbv}", file=sys.stderr)
+            return 1, 0
+        rows = sum(1 for _ in bbv.open())
+
+        # Read N_total from the drained state file's minstret field

+### F14 - `./hsim estimate` --icount 0 (drain at program exit)
+
+- Status: done
+- Type: structural (factor `on_handoff` drain body into a reusable helper)
+  + behavioral (plugin `drain_at_exit=1` mode + atexit cb + plugin-side insn
+  counter; hsim `cmd_estimate` default `--icount 0` + new helper). Lands as
+  two commits in TDD order: structural first (no behavior change), behavioral
+  second.
+- Description: Today `./hsim estimate <elf>` requires the user to know
+  N_total = `args.icount` upfront. For typical workloads (e.g. user's
+  `gemm.elf`) this is impractical. F14 makes `./hsim estimate gemm.elf`
+  "just work" by running the workload to its natural semihosting EXIT and
+  reading N_total from the drained state.
+  - Plugin: new mode `drain_at_exit=1` registers a per-insn exec callback
+    that increments a global counter, plus a
+    `qemu_plugin_register_atexit_cb` that drains state (overriding
+    `s.minstret` with the plugin counter so it's a true retired-insn count,
+    not QEMU's host-tick PMU value) and `_exit(200)`. A `drained_already`
+    guard ensures no double-drain when a CSR/PC/icount trigger fires first.
+  - hsim: `cmd_estimate`'s `--icount` default changes `10000` -> `0`. New
+    helper `_run_estimate_profile_pass(...)` returns `(rc, n_total)`:
+    icount>0 keeps the legacy path (calls `cmd_profile`, n_total=icount);
+    icount=0 inlines the profile-pass argv with `drain_at_exit=1`, reads
+    `minstret` from the drained state file via `orchestrator.abi.HybridStateV1`,
+    returns that as n_total.

diff --git a/docs/USER_GUIDE.md b/docs/USER_GUIDE.md
index 5bf7bc5..4bc19eb 100644
--- a/docs/USER_GUIDE.md
+++ b/docs/USER_GUIDE.md
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-# Hybrid Simulator User Guide
+[#](#) Hybrid Simulator User Guide
 
 Audience: a new engineer who wants to RUN the Andes hybrid simulator,
 not study its internals. After this guide you can:
@@ -678,6 +678,125 @@ python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint /tmp/m6m7/snap/0 \
   --workdir /tmp/m6m7/replay
 ```
 
+### 4.6.5 `./hsim estimate` - SimPoint cycle estimator
+
+`./hsim estimate <elf>` runs the full pipeline (M4 BBV profile -> SimPoint
+cluster -> per-phase QEMU+vsim drain) and aggregates a weighted CPI to
+project a cycle count for the whole program:
+
+```sh
+./hsim estimate gemm.elf --slice 5000 --max-k 4
+```
+
+Default `--icount 0` means **run the program to its natural exit** (e.g.
+libgloss_vh semihosting EXIT). The plugin counts every retired insn via a
+per-insn TCG callback and writes that count into the drained state file's
+`minstret` field; hsim uses it as `N_total` in:
+
+```
+CPI_i           = delta_mcycle_i / vsim_icount
+weighted_CPI    = sum_i ( w_i * CPI_i )
+estimated_cycles = N_total * weighted_CPI
+```
+
+Pass `--icount N` (positive) to instead cap the profile pass at N insns
+and use N as the multiplier (useful for very long-running workloads
+where you only want to characterize the first N insns).
+
+Example output (gemm.elf, ~254K insns):
+
+```
+profile: 4 BBV rows -> .../profile.bb (N_total=254391)
+cluster: 4 phases
+phase K=0 w=0.020 delta_mcycle=48105 CPI=9.621
+phase K=4 w=0.940 delta_mcycle=58621 CPI=11.724
+...
+estimated_cycles=2955387  (N=254391, weighted_CPI=11.617)
+```
+
+Caveats:
+
+- The fixture must reach `_exit` (return from `main`, or call `exit(0)`).
+  Programs that hang in an infinite loop will hit the QEMU 30s profile
+  timeout; pass `--icount N` instead.
+- The ELF must be linked at the andes_ae350 `-bios` load address
+  (`0x00100000`); use the linker script `tests/fixtures/test.ld` or the
+  fixture Makefile. ELFs at other addresses won't execute.
+
+#### How to pick `--slice` and `--max-k`
+
+These two flags trade profile granularity against vsim cost. Here is the
+short version; the rest of this section explains why.
+
+```
+slice  ~= N_total / 20         # aim for ~20 BBV rows
+max-k  =  2                    # start simple, increase only if needed
+```
+
+**Two-step workflow.** First run with defaults to learn N_total, then
+re-run with a tuned slice:
+
+```sh
+./hsim estimate myprog.elf
+# read the line:  profile: <rows> BBV rows ... (N_total=<N>)
+# then:
+./hsim estimate myprog.elf --slice $((N/20))
+```
+
+**Picking `--slice`** (insns per BBV row). One BBV row is the smallest

+```
+
+Pass `--icount N` (positive) to instead cap the profile pass at N insns
+and use N as the multiplier (useful for very long-running workloads
+where you only want to characterize the first N insns).
+
+Example output (gemm.elf, ~254K insns):
+
+```
+profile: 4 BBV rows -> .../profile.bb (N_total=254391)
+cluster: 4 phases
+phase K=0 w=0.020 delta_mcycle=48105 CPI=9.621
+phase K=4 w=0.940 delta_mcycle=58621 CPI=11.724
+...
+estimated_cycles=2955387  (N=254391, weighted_CPI=11.617)
+```
+
+Caveats:
+
+- The fixture must reach `_exit` (return from `main`, or call `exit(0)`).
+  Programs that hang in an infinite loop will hit the QEMU 30s profile
+  timeout; pass `--icount N` instead.
+- The ELF must be linked at the andes_ae350 `-bios` load address
+  (`0x00100000`); use the linker script `tests/fixtures/test.ld` or the
+  fixture Makefile. ELFs at other addresses won't execute.
+
+#### How to pick `--slice` and `--max-k`
+
+These two flags trade profile granularity against vsim cost. Here is the
+short version; the rest of this section explains why.
+
+```
+slice  ~= N_total / 20         # aim for ~20 BBV rows
+max-k  =  2                    # start simple, increase only if needed
+```
+
+**Two-step workflow.** First run with defaults to learn N_total, then
+re-run with a tuned slice:
+
+```sh
+./hsim estimate myprog.elf
+# read the line:  profile: <rows> BBV rows ... (N_total=<N>)
+# then:
+./hsim estimate myprog.elf --slice $((N/20))
+```
+
+**Picking `--slice`** (insns per BBV row). One BBV row is the smallest

input.content:
# Code/Test/Doc Inconsistency Review

## Context

The user asked to review all code, test cases, and documents for inconsistency and legacy code. The repo is mid-completion of F14 (`./hsim estimate --icount 0` = drain at program exit): the F14 ROADMAP entry says "done" but the implementing changes are still uncommitted across 6 modified files + 2 untracked test files. Three stray test artifacts (`gemm.c`, `gemm.elf`, `hello.c`) also sit at the repo root.

This review pulls together findings from three parallel audits (hsim+docs, source code, tests) plus targeted file verification. Issues fall into 4 buckets ordered by cost/value.

---

## Bucket A - Quick doc/cleanup wins (low cost)

| # | File | Issue | Fix |
|---|------|-------|-----|
| A1 | `docs/USER_GUIDE.md:1` | First line is `[#](#) Hybrid Simulator User Guide` (broken markdown link instead of `#` heading) | Replace with `# Hybrid Simulator User Guide` |
| A2 | `CLAUDE.md:24` | `./hsim run <elf> --mode csr\|pc` - missing `icount\|slice` modes (code has 4) | Update to `--mode csr\|pc\|icount\|slice` |
| A3 | `docs/USER_GUIDE.md:61` | Cheat-sheet row says `--mode csr\|pc [-v]` - same omission | Update to `--mode csr\|pc\|icount\|slice [-v]` |
| A4 | `gemm.elf` (repo root) | 56KB untracked binary build artifact | Delete + add `*.elf` rule to `.gitignore` at repo root scope |
| A5 | `hello.c` (repo root) | Untracked, malformed (`int int main(...)`) - junk stub | Delete |
| A6 | `gemm.c` (repo root) | Untracked, referenced in F14 ROADMAP as motivating example. Builds with host gcc (uses `<stdio.h>`), not a RISC-V fixture. | Decide: delete, or move to `tests/fixtures/` as a real RV fixture, or gitignore as a user-local playground |

**Verification:** `git status` clean after; `grep --mode CLAUDE.md docs/USER_GUIDE.md hsim` agrees across all 4 modes.

---

## Bucket B - F14 completion (close out in-flight feature)

The F14 ROADMAP at `driver_ROADMAP.md:498-545` lists 7 done criteria. The implementing changes are uncommitted:

```
M  docs/USER_GUIDE.md           - adds section 4.6.5 (estimate --icount 0)
M  driver_ROADMAP.md            - adds F14 entry, marked done
M  hsim                         - cmd_estimate F14 path + _run_drain_at_exit_profile
M  qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c - drain_at_exit mode + on_count_tick + atexit cb
                                  + on_handoff refactored to drain_state_to_file
M  tests/fixtures/Makefile      - adds no_trigger_exit to RT_C_FIXTURES
?? tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c       - new fixture, naturally exits
?? tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh - new RED test
```

**Per project TDD.md**: split structural from behavioral. Suggested commit order (mirrors F11/F12/F13 pattern in `git log`):

1. **structural #1**: factor `on_handoff` body into `drain_state_to_file` helper (plugin only, no behavior change). Verify all e2e tests still pass.
2. **behavioral #1 (RED)**: add `tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c` + Makefile entry + RED test `tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh`. Confirm test FAILS for the expected reason (no F14 logic yet).
3. **behavioral #2 (GREEN)**: add plugin `drain_at_exit_mode` + atexit cb + insn counter; change hsim `cmd_estimate` default `--icount 0` + helper. Test goes green.
4. **structural #2**: USER_GUIDE.md section 4.6.5 + ROADMAP F14 entry.

**Open sub-items inside F14:**
- B7 `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` plugin-args table at section 2.3 (lines ~315-323) does NOT list `drain_at_exit=1`. Add row: `| drain_at_exit=1 | F14: drain on program exit; overrides minstret with plugin per-insn counter |`.
- B8 Register `tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh` in a CI harness. Currently `scripts/run_e2e.sh` does not run anything under `tests/hsim/`. Either:
  - add a `tests/hsim/run_all.sh` wrapper to be invoked by `./hsim test`, OR
  - document as manual-only and note in the ROADMAP F14 done criteria.
- B9 Consider renaming fixture to `rt_c_no_trigger_exit` for consistency with the `rt_c_*` family (all other C-level fixtures follow this prefix). The Makefile `RT_C_FIXTURES` currently lumps it in without the prefix.

---

## Bucket C - Architectural doc gaps (CLAUDE.md)

These are real design choices that need a single-line note so a future maintainer doesn't think they're bugs.

| # | Topic | Where to document |
|---|-------|-------------------|
| C1 | QEMU plugin drains 11 M-mode CSRs (mepc, mcause, mtval, mscratch, medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, satp, mcycle, minstret). ResumeDriver restores only 4 (mstatus, mtvec, mcycle, minstret). The other 7 are restored via QEMU2's gdbstub. Intentional - vsim phase doesn't execute trap-taking code - but invisible. | Add to CLAUDE.md "Three-Phase Handoff" section: a one-line note "Trap CSRs (mepc/mcause/mtval/mscratch/medeleg/mideleg/mip/mie/satp) are restored only on QEMU2 via gdbstub; vsim phase is assumed trap-free." |
| C2 | PMP CSRs drained by QEMU1, restored by QEMU2 gdbstub. vsim StateDrain/ResumeDriver ignore PMP entirely. | Same section, one sentence: "PMP (64 pmpaddr + 16 pmpcfg) is also QEMU2-only." |
| C3 | `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` doesn't document wire-format endianness. Native LE assumed. | One comment line above struct definition: "Multi-byte fields are little-endian (native on RV64 + x86-64 host)." |

---

## Bucket D - Test coverage gaps

| # | File | Status |
|---|------|--------|
| D1 | `verilator/tests/hybrid/program_buffer.test.cpp` | Exists but NOT registered in `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`. Add to the doctest registration block alongside `csr_trigger.test.cpp`, `icount_trigger.test.cpp`, etc. |
| D2 | `verilator/src/hybrid/exit_codes.hpp` | Pure constants header. No tests needed. |
| D3 | `verilator/src/hybrid/dmi_poll.hpp` | No `*.test.cpp`. Decide: test directly, or accept it as covered by `resume_driver.test.cpp` / `state_drain.test.cpp` callers. |
| D4 | `verilator/src/hybrid/gdbstub_session.hpp` | Tested indirectly via `gdbstub_client.test.cpp` + `RecordingSession` in `qemu_handback.test.cpp`. Acceptable. |
| D5 | `verilator/src/hybrid/systemc_dmi_bus.hpp` | SystemC-coupled; can only test in integration. Acceptable. |

---

## Bucket E - Items I deliberately did NOT flag

For transparency, items the audit raised that I'm leaving alone:

- `volatile drained_already` in plugin: POSIX atexit runs after threads join, fine.
- `fprintf(stderr, ...)` in plugin/handback: informational, useful in test logs.
- Stale "unused insn" comment in `csr_trigger.hpp:62`: confirmed `insn` IS unused by callers; comment is correct.
- No `TODO`/`FIXME`/`HACK` markers anywhere in `qemu_plugin/`, `verilator/src/hybrid/`, `scripts/`, or `hsim`. Clean.

---

## Recommended ordering

If we tackle everything: A -> B -> C -> D. Bucket A is independent and trivial (~10 min total). Bucket B is the real work (proper TDD split, ~30 min). Buckets C and D are doc-only follow-ups.

A safer split: do A and B in this session (cleanup + close F14 properly), then defer C/D to a separate review session so commits stay focused.

## Critical files referenced

- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hsim` (driver)
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` (plugin)
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/CLAUDE.md`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/driver_ROADMAP.md`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tests/fixtures/Makefile`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi.h`

## Verification

- `git status` clean (no untracked files in repo root, all M files committed).
- `git log --oneline -10` shows 4 F14 commits (2 structural, 2 behavioral) per TDD order.
- `./hsim test --filter no_trigger_exit` and `./hsim estimate tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.elf --slice 1000 --max-k 2` both pass.
- `JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh` still 45 PASS / 0 FAIL.
- `bash tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh` passes.
- `grep -n "csr|pc[^|]" CLAUDE.md docs/USER_GUIDE.md` returns nothing outside intentional historical references.

# Code/Test/Doc Inconsistency Review

## Context

The user asked to review all code, test cases, and documents for inconsistency and legacy code. The repo is mid-completion of F14 (`./hsim estimate --icount 0` = drain at program exit): the F14 ROADMAP entry says "done" but the implementing changes are still uncommitted across 6 modified files + 2 untracked test files. Three stray test artifacts (`gemm.c`, `gemm.elf`, `hello.c`) also sit at the repo root.

This review pulls together findings from three parallel audits (hsim+docs, source code, tests) plus targeted file verification. Issues fall into 4 buckets ordered by cost/value.

---

## Bucket A - Quick doc/cleanup wins (low cost)

| # | File | Issue | Fix |
|---|------|-------|-----|
| A1 | `docs/USER_GUIDE.md:1` | First line is `[#](#) Hybrid Simulator User Guide` (broken markdown link instead of `#` heading) | Replace with `# Hybrid Simulator User Guide` |
| A2 | `CLAUDE.md:24` | `./hsim run <elf> --mode csr\|pc` - missing `icount\|slice` modes (code has 4) | Update to `--mode csr\|pc\|icount\|slice` |
| A3 | `docs/USER_GUIDE.md:61` | Cheat-sheet row says `--mode csr\|pc [-v]` - same omission | Update to `--mode csr\|pc\|icount\|slice [-v]` |
| A4 | `gemm.elf` (repo root) | 56KB untracked binary build artifact | Delete + add `*.elf` rule to `.gitignore` at repo root scope |
| A5 | `hello.c` (repo root) | Untracked, malformed (`int int main(...)`) - junk stub | Delete |
| A6 | `gemm.c` (repo root) | Untracked, referenced in F14 ROADMAP as motivating example. Builds with host gcc (uses `<stdio.h>`), not a RISC-V fixture. | Decide: delete, or move to `tests/fixtures/` as a real RV fixture, or gitignore as a user-local playground |

**Verification:** `git status` clean after; `grep --mode CLAUDE.md docs/USER_GUIDE.md hsim` agrees across all 4 modes.

---

## Bucket B - F14 completion (close out in-flight feature)

The F14 ROADMAP at `driver_ROADMAP.md:498-545` lists 7 done criteria. The implementing changes are uncommitted:

```
M  docs/USER_GUIDE.md           - adds section 4.6.5 (estimate --icount 0)
M  driver_ROADMAP.md            - adds F14 entry, marked done
M  hsim                         - cmd_estimate F14 path + _run_drain_at_exit_profile
M  qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c - drain_at_exit mode + on_count_tick + atexit cb
                                  + on_handoff refactored to drain_state_to_file
M  tests/fixtures/Makefile      - adds no_trigger_exit to RT_C_FIXTURES
?? tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c       - new fixture, naturally exits
?? tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh - new RED test
```

**Per project TDD.md**: split structural from behavioral. Suggested commit order (mirrors F11/F12/F13 pattern in `git log`):

1. **structural #1**: factor `on_handoff` body into `drain_state_to_file` helper (plugin only, no behavior change). Verify all e2e tests still pass.
2. **behavioral #1 (RED)**: add `tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c` + Makefile entry + RED test `tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh`. Confirm test FAILS for the expected reason (no F14 logic yet).
3. **behavioral #2 (GREEN)**: add plugin `drain_at_exit_mode` + atexit cb + insn counter; change hsim `cmd_estimate` default `--icount 0` + helper. Test goes green.
4. **structural #2**: USER_GUIDE.md section 4.6.5 + ROADMAP F14 entry.

**Open sub-items inside F14:**
- B7 `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` plugin-args table at section 2.3 (lines ~315-323) does NOT list `drain_at_exit=1`. Add row: `| drain_at_exit=1 | F14: drain on program exit; overrides minstret with plugin per-insn counter |`.
- B8 Register `tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh` in a CI harness. Currently `scripts/run_e2e.sh` does not run anything under `tests/hsim/`. Either:
  - add a `tests/hsim/run_all.sh` wrapper to be invoked by `./hsim test`, OR
  - document as manual-only and note in the ROADMAP F14 done criteria.
- B9 Consider renaming fixture to `rt_c_no_trigger_exit` for consistency with the `rt_c_*` family (all other C-level fixtures follow this prefix). The Makefile `RT_C_FIXTURES` currently lumps it in without the prefix.

---

## Bucket C - Architectural doc gaps (CLAUDE.md)

These are real design choices that need a single-line note so a future maintainer doesn't think they're bugs.

| # | Topic | Where to document |
|---|-------|-------------------|
| C1 | QEMU plugin drains 11 M-mode CSRs (mepc, mcause, mtval, mscratch, medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, satp, mcycle, minstret). ResumeDriver restores only 4 (mstatus, mtvec, mcycle, minstret). The other 7 are restored via QEMU2's gdbstub. Intentional - vsim phase doesn't execute trap-taking code - but invisible. | Add to CLAUDE.md "Three-Phase Handoff" section: a one-line note "Trap CSRs (mepc/mcause/mtval/mscratch/medeleg/mideleg/mip/mie/satp) are restored only on QEMU2 via gdbstub; vsim phase is assumed trap-free." |
| C2 | PMP CSRs drained by QEMU1, restored by QEMU2 gdbstub. vsim StateDrain/ResumeDriver ignore PMP entirely. | Same section, one sentence: "PMP (64 pmpaddr + 16 pmpcfg) is also QEMU2-only." |
| C3 | `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` doesn't document wire-format endianness. Native LE assumed. | One comment line above struct definition: "Multi-byte fields are little-endian (native on RV64 + x86-64 host)." |

---

## Bucket D - Test coverage gaps

| # | File | Status |
|---|------|--------|
| D1 | `verilator/tests/hybrid/program_buffer.test.cpp` | Exists but NOT registered in `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`. Add to the doctest registration block alongside `csr_trigger.test.cpp`, `icount_trigger.test.cpp`, etc. |
| D2 | `verilator/src/hybrid/exit_codes.hpp` | Pure constants header. No tests needed. |
| D3 | `verilator/src/hybrid/dmi_poll.hpp` | No `*.test.cpp`. Decide: test directly, or accept it as covered by `resume_driver.test.cpp` / `state_drain.test.cpp` callers. |
| D4 | `verilator/src/hybrid/gdbstub_session.hpp` | Tested indirectly via `gdbstub_client.test.cpp` + `RecordingSession` in `qemu_handback.test.cpp`. Acceptable. |
| D5 | `verilator/src/hybrid/systemc_dmi_bus.hpp` | SystemC-coupled; can only test in integration. Acceptable. |

---

## Bucket E - Items I deliberately did NOT flag

For transparency, items the audit raised that I'm leaving alone:

- `volatile drained_already` in plugin: POSIX atexit runs after threads join, fine.
- `fprintf(stderr, ...)` in plugin/handback: informational, useful in test logs.
- Stale "unused insn" comment in `csr_trigger.hpp:62`: confirmed `insn` IS unused by callers; comment is correct.
- No `TODO`/`FIXME`/`HACK` markers anywhere in `qemu_plugin/`, `verilator/src/hybrid/`, `scripts/`, or `hsim`. Clean.

---

## Recommended ordering

If we tackle everything: A -> B -> C -> D. Bucket A is independent and trivial (~10 min total). Bucket B is the real work (proper TDD split, ~30 min). Buckets C and D are doc-only follow-ups.

A safer split: do A and B in this session (cleanup + close F14 properly), then defer C/D to a separate review session so commits stay focused.

## Critical files referenced

- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hsim` (driver)
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` (plugin)
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/CLAUDE.md`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/driver_ROADMAP.md`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tests/fixtures/Makefile`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
- `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/state_abi.h`

## Verification

# Code/Test/Doc Inconsistency Review

## Context

The user asked to review all code, test cases, and documents for inconsistency and legacy code. The repo is mid-completion of F14 (`./hsim estimate --icount 0` = drain at program exit): the F14 ROADMAP entry says "done" but the implementing changes are still uncommitted across 6 modified files + 2 untracked test files. Three stray test artifacts (`gemm.c`, `gemm.elf`, `hello.c`) also sit at the repo root.

This review pulls together findings from three parallel audits (hsim+docs, source code, tests) plus targeted file verification. Issues fall into 4 buckets ordered by cost/value.

---

## Bucket A - Quick doc/cleanup wins (low cost)

| # | File | Issue | Fix |
|---|------|-------|-----|
| A1 | `docs/USER_GUIDE.md:1` | First line is `[#](#) Hybrid Simulator User Guide` (broken markdown link instead of `#` heading) | Replace with `# Hybrid Simulator User Guide` |
| A2 | `CLAUDE.md:24` | `./hsim run <elf> --mode csr\|pc` - missing `icount\|slice` modes (code has 4) | Update to `--mode csr\|pc\|icount\|slice` |
| A3 | `docs/USER_GUIDE.md:61` | Cheat-sheet row says `--mode csr\|pc [-v]` - same omission | Update to `--mode csr\|pc\|icount\|slice [-v]` |
| A4 | `gemm.elf` (repo root) | 56KB untracked binary build artifact | Delete + add `*.elf` rule to `.gitignore` at repo root scope |
| A5 | `hello.c` (repo root) | Untracked, malformed (`int int main(...)`) - junk stub | Delete |
| A6 | `gemm.c` (repo root) | Untracked, referenced in F14 ROADMAP as narrative-only motivation. Builds with host gcc, not a RV fixture. | Delete (per user). |

**Verification:** `git status` clean after; `grep --mode CLAUDE.md docs/USER_GUIDE.md hsim` agrees across all 4 modes.

---

## Bucket B - F14 completion (close out in-flight feature)

The F14 ROADMAP at `driver_ROADMAP.md:498-545` lists 7 done criteria. The implementing changes are uncommitted:

```
M  docs/USER_GUIDE.md           - adds section 4.6.5 (estimate --icount 0)
M  driver_ROADMAP.md            - adds F14 entry, marked done
M  hsim                         - cmd_estimate F14 path + _run_drain_at_exit_profile
M  qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c - drain_at_exit mode + on_count_tick + atexit cb
                                  + on_handoff refactored to drain_state_to_file
M  tests/fixtures/Makefile      - adds no_trigger_exit to RT_C_FIXTURES
?? tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c       - new fixture, naturally exits
?? tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh - new RED test
```

**Per project TDD.md**: split structural from behavioral. Suggested commit order (mirrors F11/F12/F13 pattern in `git log`):

1. **structural #1**: factor `on_handoff` body into `drain_state_to_file` helper (plugin only, no behavior change). Verify all e2e tests still pass.
2. **behavioral #1 (RED)**: add `tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.c` + Makefile entry + RED test `tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh`. Confirm test FAILS for the expected reason (no F14 logic yet).
3. **behavioral #2 (GREEN)**: add plugin `drain_at_exit_mode` + atexit cb + insn counter; change hsim `cmd_estimate` default `--icount 0` + helper. Test goes green.
4. **structural #2**: USER_GUIDE.md section 4.6.5 + ROADMAP F14 entry.

**Open sub-items inside F14:**
- B7 `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` plugin-args table at section 2.3 (lines ~315-323) does NOT list `drain_at_exit=1`. Add row: `| drain_at_exit=1 | F14: drain on program exit; overrides minstret with plugin per-insn counter |`.
- B8 Register `tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh` in a CI harness. Currently `scripts/run_e2e.sh` does not run anything under `tests/hsim/`. Either:
  - add a `tests/hsim/run_all.sh` wrapper to be invoked by `./hsim test`, OR
  - document as manual-only and note in the ROADMAP F14 done criteria.
- B9 Consider renaming fixture to `rt_c_no_trigger_exit` for consistency with the `rt_c_*` family (all other C-level fixtures follow this prefix). The Makefile `RT_C_FIXTURES` currently lumps it in without the prefix.

---

## Bucket C - Architectural doc gaps (CLAUDE.md)

These are real design choices that need a single-line note so a future maintainer doesn't think they're bugs.

| # | Topic | Where to document |
|---|-------|-------------------|
| C1 | QEMU plugin drains 11 M-mode CSRs (mepc, mcause, mtval, mscratch, medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, satp, mcycle, minstret). ResumeDriver restores only 4 (mstatus, mtvec, mcycle, minstret). The other 7 are restored via QEMU2's gdbstub. Intentional - vsim phase doesn't execute trap-taking code - but invisible. | Add to CLAUDE.md "Three-Phase Handoff" section: a one-line note "Trap CSRs (mepc/mcause/mtval/mscratch/medeleg/mideleg/mip/mie/satp) are restored only on QEMU2 via gdbstub; vsim phase is assumed trap-free." |
| C2 | PMP CSRs drained by QEMU1, restored by QEMU2 gdbstub. vsim StateDrain/ResumeDriver ignore PMP entirely. | Same section, one sentence: "PMP (64 pmpaddr + 16 pmpcfg) is also QEMU2-only." |
| C3 | `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` doesn't document wire-format endianness. Native LE assumed. | One comment line above struct definition: "Multi-byte fields are little-endian (native on RV64 + x86-64 host)." |

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
* Always update docs/USER_GUIDE.md if something changed.

## Repository Overview

Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz-level) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) share architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The system runs a three-phase round-trip: **QEMU1** (drain on CSR/PC trigger) -> **vsim** (resume + execute + drain) -> **QEMU2** (restore via gdbstub, continue).

Two Git submodules:
- `QEMU/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`)
- `verilator/` - vsim sources; `hybrid/` subdirs hold the plugin and test fixtures
- `tools/simpoint/` - SimPoint 3.2 (UCSD) clustering binary; pinned to upstream `17783e5`

Shared ABI lives at `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (not inside either submodule).

## Build Commands

**Primary path (`./hsim`)** -- the end-user driver, used in all user-facing docs:

```sh
./hsim build                       # builds qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint, fixtures
./hsim test                        # full e2e suite (defaults: MODE=both, JOBS=8)
./hsim run <elf> --mode csr|pc     # one round-trip
./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>
```

**Developer escape hatch** -- the underlying scripts. Use these when
working on the build system, the harness, or a single leg in isolation:

```sh
# Build everything (qemu -> vsim -> plugin -> simpoint)
source ./setup.sh

# Build individual components
./scripts/build_qemu.sh                              # QEMU (configure + make -j64)
./scripts/build_vsim.sh                              # vsim (Docker/podman, runs ./build.sh inside container)
make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU  # TCG plugin (out-of-tree)
./scripts/build_simpoint.sh                          # SimPoint clustering binary (g++ on host)

# Build RISC-V ELF test fixtures
make -C tests/fixtures              # all fixtures
make -C tests/fixtures rt_c_v_regs.elf  # single fixture

# Clean
source ./setup.sh clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all]
```

**Outputs:**
- `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium` (or other CPU variant)
- `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`
- `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`

`scripts/build_vsim.sh` is Docker-based (podman). It builds an image from `verilator/Dockerfile` (target `env`) then runs `./build.sh` inside it, mounting the repo at `/work` and `include/` as `/include:ro`.

## Running Tests

```sh
# All e2e tests in parallel (auto-detects job count from nproc + /dev/shm headroom)
bash scripts/run_e2e.sh

# Run with specific options
JOBS=4 MODE=csr bash scripts/run_e2e.sh         # 4-way parallel, CSR-mode only
JOBS=1 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh                  # sequential (debug)
FILTER=rt_c_v bash scripts/run_e2e.sh           # regex filter on fixture name
LOG_DIR=/tmp/my-logs bash scripts/run_e2e.sh    # custom log directory

# Unit tests (inside vsim build container)
# The container build runs: cmake --build build -j
# To run ctest directly after build:
#   podman run ... ./build.sh && podman exec ... ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
```

**Important:** on a high-core-count machine (128+ cores) `default_jobs()` computes `min(nproc, /dev/shm_free/256MB)` which can reach 128+. Running that many vsim Verilator processes simultaneously saturates CPU and causes timeouts. Use `JOBS=8` (or lower) as a practical cap: `JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh`.

`scripts/run_e2e.sh` exits 0 only when all tests pass. Logs go to a `mktemp` directory under `/tmp`; path is printed at the start of each run. Each test's full output lands in `$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log`.

## Architecture

### Three-Phase Handoff

```
QEMU1 --(state file)--> vsim --(state file + V-scratch mmap)--> QEMU2
```

1. **QEMU plugin** (`qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`) - TCG plugin. On CSR-write trigger (`csrwi 0x7C0, 0`) or PC-trigger, drains QEMU's architectural state into a binary `hybrid_state_v1` struct written to `--outfile`.
2. **vsim handoff controller** (`src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp`) - Detects the trigger inside vsim, pauses the hart via DMI, then calls `ResumeDriver` to restore state.
3. **ResumeDriver** (`src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`) - Writes GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-state into the vsim hart via RISC-V Debug Module Abstract Commands and Program Buffer.
4. **StateDrain** (`src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`) - On vsim exit trigger, reads back the full architectural state via DMI Abstract Commands.
5. **qemu_handback** (`src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`) - Spawns QEMU2, connects via gdbstub, sends P-packet register writes for GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-regs, then `continue`s.

### Trigger Modes

- **CSR mode** (default): Pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (entry) / `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (exit) at retire time. CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on Andes ax45mp.
- **PC mode**: ELF symbols `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` resolved at startup; CSR insns are bypassed.

Both modes coexist in the fixtures. `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc` selects at runtime.

### Shared ABI (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`)

`hybrid_state_v1` is the binary wire format exchanged between QEMU plugin, vsim, and the handback path. It holds: 32 GPRs, 32 FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, etc.), PMP (64 pmpaddr + 16 pmpcfg), and the V extension block (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, 32 vreg[], vxsat, vxrm, vcsr). `HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64` supports VLEN up to 512 bits.

Flags (`HYBRID_FLAG_RV64`, `HYBRID_FLAG_FP`, `HYBRID_FLAG_V`) gate which state blocks are live. The ABI struct is validated by `tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp`.

### V-State Transport

V register transport uses a fixed scratch region (`kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`) in the shared mmap. vsim's inline asm writes all 32 V regs there via `vse8.v`; `StateDrain` reads those bytes into `hybrid_state_v1::vreg[]`. `qemu_handback` ships them to QEMU2 via gdbstub P-packets by register number.

V scalar CSR transport (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr):
- **Tier A** (Abstract Commands): vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, mstatus.VS
- **Tier B** (Program Buffer): vtype + vl via `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` (direct CSR write to vtype sets vill; vl is read-only; only `vsetvl` restores both atomically)

Restore order in `ResumeDriver::resume()` when `HYBRID_FLAG_V` is set:
1. `mstatus.VS` (enables V in the hart)
2. `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in Program Buffer (stages `vl` in x5, `vtype` in x6 via abstract GPR writes first)
3. `vstart` (AFTER vsetvl: vsetvl resets vstart to 0)
4. `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`
5. GPR loop (overwrites the x5/x6 scratch - safe now)

### Test Fixtures

All fixtures run on the unified `tests/hybrid/e2e.sh` harness, which
sets up an `/dev/shm`-backed shared mmap (128 MB) so memory survives
the QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 round-trip. Two fixture families:

| Family   | Examples                                                    | Notes |
|----------|-------------------------------------------------------------|-------|
| asm      | `handoff_roundtrip`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, `rt_shared_mem` | no C runtime; don't strictly need shared mmap but use it anyway (~20 ms cost is negligible). |
| C-level  | `rt_c_hello`, `rt_c_matmul`, `rt_c_v_regs`, `rt_c_v_csrs`   | linked against vsim-demo C runtime. Shared mmap is REQUIRED -- the stack/heap and `main()`'s ra-spill/restore around `csrwi 0x7C0,1` must survive vsim -> QEMU2. |

The shared mmap is created with `mktemp -p /dev/shm` + `truncate`,
then QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file`.

Fixture source lives in `tests/fixtures/`. C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c}` plus the Andes toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting-backed libgloss), using `rt_c.ld`. V-using fixtures require `-march=rv64gcv`. **Phase-2 rule:** the span between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()` runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (no `printf`, `fopen`, semihosting ebreak, etc.) -- vsim does not implement semihosting, so any libgloss-routed call inside the ROI traps. Phase 1 and phase 3 print via standard newlib stdio (both QEMUs are launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`).

### vsim CMake Structure

vsim lives entirely in `verilator/`. Key CMake options:
- `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID` (default OFF) - enables e2e CTest registration
- `VSIM_CONFIG_FILE` - CPU YAML (default `config.yaml`)
- `VSIM_NO_VERILATOR` - skip Verilator build (assume prebuilt)

The hybrid orchestration layer (`src/hybrid/*.hpp`) is header-only and depends only on C++20 standard library + the shared ABI header. SystemC/Verilator are not required for hybrid unit tests.

Hybrid unit tests (`tests/hybrid/`) use [doctest](https://github.com/doctest/doctest). They are registered in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` and run via `ctest`. The `recording_bus.hpp` / `RecordingSession` patterns provide duck-typed test doubles for the DMI bus and gdbstub session without requiring virtual interfaces.

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` | Wire-format struct (shared by all three components) |
| `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` | TCG plugin: drain on trigger |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` | Phase dispatcher |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` | DMI state restore (Tier A+B) |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` | DMI state capture |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` | Spawn QEMU2, gdbstub P-packets |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` | V register transport via scratch region |
| `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` | CTest registration for all e2e fixtures |
| `scripts/run_e2e.sh` | Parallel e2e runner (FIFO job pool, per-test logs) |
| `tests/fixtures/Makefile` | RISC-V ELF fixture build (Andes toolchain) |

attachment.snippet:
1	# Hybrid Simulator User Guide
2	
3	Audience: a new engineer who wants to RUN the Andes hybrid simulator,
4	not study its internals. After this guide you can:
5	
6	1. Build every artifact from a fresh clone.
7	2. Configure QEMU, vsim, and the orchestrator for a workload.
8	3. Pick a switching mode (M1..M8) and run one round-trip end-to-end
9	   with a concrete worked example.
10	
11	For internals, read `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md`. For per-mode drill-down,
12	read `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md`.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## 0. The one-paragraph mental model
17	
18	QEMU runs the boring parts at MHz. vsim (Verilator + SystemC) runs the
19	small region-of-interest at cycle accuracy. They share RAM via an
20	`mmap` file. A magic trigger (CSR write, PC, or instruction count)
21	hands control between them. One run is three legs:
22	
23	```
24	   QEMU1  -- state.bin + shared mmap -->  vsim  -- same files -->  QEMU2
25	   (fast)                                 (cycle accurate)         (fast)
26	```
27	
28	The thing you choose per run is the TRIGGER that decides where each
29	leg drains and the next leg resumes. That choice is the "switching
30	mode".
31	
32	---
33	
34	## 0.5 Quick start with `./hsim`
35	
36	`./hsim` is the canonical end-user entry point. It wraps the
37	developer-grade scripts documented in Sections 1-5 (`setup.sh`,
38	`tests/e2e.sh`, `scripts/run_e2e.sh`) behind four subcommands.
39	
40	Four-command worked example - cold checkout to passing suite:
41	
42	```sh
43	./hsim build                                            # qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint, fixtures
44	./hsim list fixtures                                    # discover available fixtures
45	./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr     # one CSR-mode round-trip
46	./hsim test                                             # full e2e suite (defaults: MODE=both, JOBS=8)
47	```
48	
49	Expected last line of `./hsim test`:
50	
51	```
52	PASS 45, FAIL 0, SKIP 0 (elapsed: <Ns>)
53	```
54	
55	Subcommand cheat sheet:
56	
57	| Command                                                          | What it does                                                        |
58	|------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
59	| `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`                             | List available fixtures, demos, or trigger modes (M1..M8).          |
60	| `./hsim build [--what TARGET] [--dry-run]`                       | Build artifacts; targets: `all`, `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `simpoint`, `fixtures`. |
61	| `./hsim run <elf> --mode csr|pc [-v]`                            | Run one ELF through the QEMU -> vsim -> QEMU round-trip.            |
62	| `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`   | Run the e2e suite; defaults `--mode both --jobs 8`.                 |
63	
64	Run `./hsim <cmd> -h` for the full flag list of a subcommand. The rest
65	of this guide (Sections 1-7) documents the underlying scripts and is
66	useful when you need to bypass `./hsim` (custom env vars, single-leg
67	debugging, alternative CPU YAML, etc.).
68	
69	---
70	
71	## 1. Build and environment setup
72	
73	### 1.1 Host prerequisites
74	
75	Install these on the host once:
76	
77	| Package          | Notes                                                |
78	|------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
79	| `git`            | clone the repo and its submodules                    |
80	| C/C++ toolchain  | clang or gcc; needed by QEMU and the plugin          |
81	| `cmake` >= 3.20  | vsim build                                           |
82	| `ninja`          | vsim default generator                               |
83	| `python3`        | orchestrator, plugin tests, demo scripts             |
84	| `glib-2.0` dev   | QEMU plugin link line                                |
85	| `pkg-config`     | QEMU configure                                       |
86	| `podman`         | runs vsim builds inside `vsim-env:latest` container  |
87	| Verilator        | auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed |
88	| QEMU's own deps  | pixman, slirp, ... see `QEMU/README.rst`      |
89	| RISC-V toolchain | `riscv64-unknown-elf-{gcc,nm,objdump}` (Andes v5d)   |
90	
91	The Andes RISC-V toolchain is needed to build the fixtures and to
92	resolve symbols for PC-mode. The harness scripts default to
93	`/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/.../bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`;
94	override with `NM=/your/path/to/nm` if you keep it elsewhere.
95	
96	### 1.2 Clone with submodules
97	
98	```sh
99	git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
100	cd hybrid_sim
101	```
102	
103	If you forgot `--recursive`:
104	
105	```sh
106	git submodule update --init --recursive
107	```
108	
109	Submodules under this repo:
110	
111	| Path             | What it is                                          |
112	|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
113	| `QEMU/`   | QEMU 9.x fork that hosts the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`) |
114	| `verilator/`   | vsim sources (CMake + SystemC + Verilator)          |
115	| `tools/simpoint/`| SimPoint 3.2 clustering binary (UCSD)               |
116	
117	### 1.3 Per-host config (`config.env`)
118	
119	The repo ships a template `config.env.example` listing per-host paths
120	that cannot live in the build system (notably the Andes RISC-V
121	toolchain root, which `setup.sh` does NOT build).
122	
123	On the first `source ./setup.sh`, the template is copied to
124	`./config.env` (gitignored) so your edits never touch git history. If
125	the toolchain lives somewhere other than the default, edit
126	`config.env`:
127	
128	```sh
129	# config.env
130	HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
131	```
132	
133	The single variable today is:
134	
135	| Variable          | What it points at                                                  |
136	|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
137	| `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN`| Toolchain root. `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/` must contain `riscv64-unknown-elf-{gcc,ld,nm,objdump,...}`. |
138	
139	How it propagates:
140	
141	- `setup.sh` sources `config.env`, validates `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/`
142	  exists, and `export`s the variable so all child processes see it.
143	- The fixture `Makefile` (`tests/fixtures/Makefile`) defaults
144	  `TOOLCHAIN` to `$(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)` and aborts with a clear error if
145	  the variable is missing.
146	- The unified `e2e.sh` test harness loads `config.env` itself when run
147	  without prior `source ./setup.sh`, then derives
148	  `NM=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`.
149	
150	Override per-invocation without editing the file:
151	
152	```sh
153	HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/opt/my-toolchain make -C tests/fixtures
154	HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/opt/my-toolchain bash tests/demos/demo_pc_oneshot.sh
155	```
156	
157	### 1.4 Build everything
158	
159	One command builds all four artifacts and exports the env vars the
160	test harness expects:
161	
162	```sh
163	source ./setup.sh
164	```
165	
166	`source` (not `bash`) is required so the script can prepend the build
167	dirs to PATH and export `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_BIN`,
168	`SIMPOINT_BIN`, `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` into the current shell. The steps it
169	runs:
170	
171	```
172	0/4 config     -> auto-copy config.env.example -> config.env (first run);
173	                  source it; export HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN
174	1/4 QEMU       -> build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
175	2/4 vsim       -> verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium    (in container)
176	3/4 plugin     -> qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
177	4/4 SimPoint   -> tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
178	```
179	
180	To rebuild just one component:
181	
182	```sh
183	./scripts/build_qemu.sh
184	./scripts/build_vsim.sh        # uses podman + Dockerfile (target `env`)
185	make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU
186	./scripts/build_simpoint.sh
187	```
188	
189	To clean:
190	
191	```sh
192	source ./setup.sh clean         # all four
193	source ./setup.sh clean qemu    # just QEMU build/

... [578 lines truncated] ...
