attachment.snippet:
1	# Hybrid Simulator User Guide
2	
3	Audience: a new engineer who wants to RUN the Andes hybrid simulator,
4	not study its internals. After this guide you can:
5	
6	1. Build every artifact from a fresh clone.
7	2. Configure QEMU, vsim, and the orchestrator for a workload.
8	3. Pick a switching mode (M1..M8) and run one round-trip end-to-end
9	   with a concrete worked example.
10	
11	For internals, read `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md`. For per-mode drill-down,
12	read `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md`.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## 0. The one-paragraph mental model
17	
18	QEMU runs the boring parts at MHz. vsim (Verilator + SystemC) runs the
19	small region-of-interest at cycle accuracy. They share RAM via an
20	`mmap` file. A magic trigger (CSR write, PC, or instruction count)
21	hands control between them. One run is three legs:
22	
23	```
24	   QEMU1  -- state.bin + shared mmap -->  vsim  -- same files -->  QEMU2
25	   (fast)                                 (cycle accurate)         (fast)
26	```
27	
28	The thing you choose per run is the TRIGGER that decides where each
29	leg drains and the next leg resumes. That choice is the "switching
30	mode".
31	
32	---
33	
34	## 0.5 Quick start with `./hsim`
35	
36	`./hsim` is the canonical end-user entry point. It wraps the
37	developer-grade scripts documented in Sections 1-5 (`setup.sh`,
38	`tests/e2e.sh`, `scripts/run_e2e.sh`) behind four subcommands.
39	
40	Four-command worked example - cold checkout to passing suite:
41	
42	```sh
43	./hsim build                                            # qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint, fixtures
44	./hsim list fixtures                                    # discover available fixtures
45	./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr     # one CSR-mode round-trip
46	./hsim test                                             # full e2e suite (defaults: MODE=both, JOBS=8)
47	```
48	
49	Expected last line of `./hsim test`:
50	
51	```
52	PASS 45, FAIL 0, SKIP 0 (elapsed: <Ns>)
53	```
54	
55	Subcommand cheat sheet:
56	
57	| Command                                                          | What it does                                                        |
58	|------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
59	| `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`                             | List available fixtures, demos, or trigger modes (M1..M8).          |
60	| `./hsim build [--what TARGET] [--dry-run]`                       | Build artifacts; targets: `all`, `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `simpoint`, `fixtures`. |
61	| `./hsim run <elf> --mode csr|pc|icount|slice [-v]`               | Run one ELF through the QEMU -> vsim -> QEMU round-trip.            |
62	| `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`   | Run the e2e suite; defaults `--mode both --jobs 8`.                 |
63	
64	Run `./hsim <cmd> -h` for the full flag list of a subcommand. The rest
65	of this guide (Sections 1-7) documents the underlying scripts and is
66	useful when you need to bypass `./hsim` (custom env vars, single-leg
67	debugging, alternative CPU YAML, etc.).
68	
69	---
70	
71	## 1. Build and environment setup
72	
73	### 1.1 Host prerequisites
74	
75	Install these on the host once:
76	
77	| Package          | Notes                                                |
78	|------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
79	| `git`            | clone the repo and its submodules                    |
80	| C/C++ toolchain  | clang or gcc; needed by QEMU and the plugin          |
81	| `cmake` >= 3.20  | vsim build                                           |
82	| `ninja`          | vsim default generator                               |
83	| `python3`        | orchestrator, plugin tests, demo scripts             |
84	| `glib-2.0` dev   | QEMU plugin link line                                |
85	| `pkg-config`     | QEMU configure                                       |
86	| `podman`         | runs vsim builds inside `vsim-env:latest` container  |
87	| Verilator        | auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed |
88	| QEMU's own deps  | pixman, slirp, ... see `QEMU/README.rst`      |
89	| RISC-V toolchain | `riscv64-unknown-elf-{gcc,nm,objdump}` (Andes v5d)   |
90	
91	The Andes RISC-V toolchain is needed to build the fixtures and to
92	resolve symbols for PC-mode. The harness scripts default to
93	`/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/.../bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`;
94	override with `NM=/your/path/to/nm` if you keep it elsewhere.
95	
96	### 1.2 Clone with submodules
97	
98	```sh
99	git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
100	cd hybrid_sim
101	```
102	
103	If you forgot `--recursive`:
104	
105	```sh
106	git submodule update --init --recursive
107	```
108	
109	Submodules under this repo:
110	
111	| Path             | What it is                                          |
112	|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
113	| `QEMU/`   | QEMU 9.x fork that hosts the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`) |
114	| `verilator/`   | vsim sources (CMake + SystemC + Verilator)          |
115	| `tools/simpoint/`| SimPoint 3.2 clustering binary (UCSD)               |
116	
117	### 1.3 Per-host config (`config.env`)
118	
119	The repo ships a template `config.env.example` listing per-host paths
120	that cannot live in the build system (notably the Andes RISC-V
121	toolchain root, which `setup.sh` does NOT build).
122	
123	On the first `source ./setup.sh`, the template is copied to
124	`./config.env` (gitignored) so your edits never touch git history. If
125	the toolchain lives somewhere other than the default, edit
126	`config.env`:
127	
128	```sh
129	# config.env
130	HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
131	```
132	
133	The single variable today is:
134	
135	| Variable          | What it points at                                                  |
136	|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
137	| `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN`| Toolchain root. `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/` must contain `riscv64-unknown-elf-{gcc,ld,nm,objdump,...}`. |
138	
139	How it propagates:
140	
141	- `setup.sh` sources `config.env`, validates `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/`
142	  exists, and `export`s the variable so all child processes see it.
143	- The fixture `Makefile` (`tests/fixtures/Makefile`) defaults
144	  `TOOLCHAIN` to `$(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)` and aborts with a clear error if
145	  the variable is missing.
146	- The unified `e2e.sh` test harness loads `config.env` itself when run
147	  without prior `source ./setup.sh`, then derives
148	  `NM=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`.
149	
150	Override per-invocation without editing the file:
151	
152	```sh
153	HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/opt/my-toolchain make -C tests/fixtures
154	HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=/opt/my-toolchain bash tests/demos/demo_pc_oneshot.sh
155	```
156	
157	### 1.4 Build everything
158	
159	One command builds all four artifacts and exports the env vars the
160	test harness expects:
161	
162	```sh
163	source ./setup.sh
164	```
165	
166	`source` (not `bash`) is required so the script can prepend the build
167	dirs to PATH and export `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_BIN`,
168	`SIMPOINT_BIN`, `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` into the current shell. The steps it
169	runs:
170	
171	```
172	0/4 config     -> auto-copy config.env.example -> config.env (first run);
173	                  source it; export HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN
174	1/4 QEMU       -> build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
175	2/4 vsim       -> verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium    (in container)
176	3/4 plugin     -> qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
177	4/4 SimPoint   -> tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
178	```
179	
180	To rebuild just one component:
181	
182	```sh
183	./scripts/build_qemu.sh
184	./scripts/build_vsim.sh        # uses podman + Dockerfile (target `env`)
185	make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU
186	./scripts/build_simpoint.sh
187	```
188	
189	To clean:
190	
191	```sh
192	source ./setup.sh clean         # all four
193	source ./setup.sh clean qemu    # just QEMU build/

... [697 lines truncated] ...

content.content:
./QEMU/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h
./QEMU/build/qemu-bundle/usr/local/include/qemu-plugin.h
---
# Out-of-tree build for the QEMU TCG plugin libhybrid_handoff.so. Standalone
# from the vsim CMake build because the plugin links against host QEMU's
# headers + glib, neither of which exist inside the vsim build container.
#
# Usage:
#   make QEMU_ROOT=/local/nick/qemu_v5
#
# Output: libhybrid_handoff.so in this directory.

QEMU_ROOT ?= /local/nick/qemu_v5
CC        ?= gcc

GLIB_CFLAGS := $(shell pkg-config --cflags glib-2.0)
GLIB_LIBS   := $(shell pkg-config --libs   glib-2.0)

CFLAGS  := -O2 -fPIC -Wall -Wextra -std=c11
HYBRID_INCLUDE ?= ../include
INCLUDES := -I$(HYBRID_INCLUDE) -I$(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu $(GLIB_CFLAGS)
LDFLAGS := -shared
LIBS    := $(GLIB_LIBS)

.PHONY: all clean

all: libhybrid_handoff.so

libhybrid_handoff.so: hybrid_handoff.c bbv_emit.c bbv_emit.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi_check.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/insn_match.h

---
# Out-of-tree build for the QEMU TCG plugin libhybrid_handoff.so. Standalone
# from the vsim CMake build because the plugin links against host QEMU's
# headers + glib, neither of which exist inside the vsim build container.
#
# Usage:
#   make QEMU_ROOT=/local/nick/qemu_v5
#
# Output: libhybrid_handoff.so in this directory.

QEMU_ROOT ?= /local/nick/qemu_v5
CC        ?= gcc

GLIB_CFLAGS := $(shell pkg-config --cflags glib-2.0)
GLIB_LIBS   := $(shell pkg-config --libs   glib-2.0)

CFLAGS  := -O2 -fPIC -Wall -Wextra -std=c11
HYBRID_INCLUDE ?= ../include
INCLUDES := -I$(HYBRID_INCLUDE) -I$(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu $(GLIB_CFLAGS)
LDFLAGS := -shared
LIBS    := $(GLIB_LIBS)

.PHONY: all clean

all: libhybrid_handoff.so

libhybrid_handoff.so: hybrid_handoff.c bbv_emit.c bbv_emit.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi_check.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/insn_match.h
	@if [ ! -f $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h ]; then \
	    echo "error: qemu-plugin.h not under $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/"; \
	    echo "       set QEMU_ROOT=<path> on the make command line"; \
	    exit 1; \
	fi
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCLUDES) $(LDFLAGS) -o $@ hybrid_handoff.c bbv_emit.c $(LIBS)

clean:
	rm -f libhybrid_handoff.so

def cmd_estimate(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    elf = Path(args.elf)
    if not elf.is_absolute():
        elf = (Path.cwd() / elf).resolve()
    if not elf.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: ELF not found: {args.elf}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    artifacts = _resolve_artifacts()
    for label, path in artifacts.items():
        if not path.is_file():
            print(f"hsim: {label} not built -- run ./hsim build",
                  file=sys.stderr)
            return 2
    if not _SIMPOINT_BIN.is_file():
        print("hsim: simpoint not built -- run ./hsim build",
              file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    # SimPoint-correct default: vsim simulates exactly one slice's
    # insns per phase, so CPI_i = delta_mcycle / slice. 0 = "auto".
    vsim_icount = args.vsim_icount if args.vsim_icount > 0 else args.slice

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="hsim-estimate-") as tmpdir:
        tmp = Path(tmpdir)
        bbv = tmp / "profile.bb"
        simpts = tmp / "simpts"
        weights = tmp / "weights"

        rc = cmd_profile(argparse.Namespace(
            elf=str(elf), slice=args.slice, icount=args.icount,
            output=str(bbv), dry_run=False,
            mmap_size=args.mmap_size,
        ))
        if rc != 0:
            return rc

        rc = cmd_cluster(argparse.Namespace(
            bbv=str(bbv), max_k=args.max_k,
            simpts=str(simpts), weights=str(weights), dry_run=False,
        ))
        if rc != 0:
            return rc

        slice_indices = [int(line.split()[0])
                         for line in simpts.read_text().splitlines()
                         if line.strip()]
        weight_vals = [float(line.split()[0])
                       for line in weights.read_text().splitlines()
                       if line.strip()]
        if len(slice_indices) != len(weight_vals):
            print(f"hsim: simpts/weights row mismatch "
                  f"({len(slice_indices)} vs {len(weight_vals)})",
                  file=sys.stderr)
            return 1

        phases = []
        for K, w in zip(slice_indices, weight_vals):
            print(f"[hsim estimate] phase slice_at={K} weight={w:.3f}",
                  flush=True)
            try:
                mc_before, mc_after = _estimate_one_phase(
                    elf, artifacts, args.slice, K,
                    args.icount, vsim_icount,
                    mmap_size=args.mmap_size,
                )
            except RuntimeError as exc:
                print(f"hsim: estimate failed: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
                return 1
            delta = mc_after - mc_before
            cpi = delta / vsim_icount if vsim_icount > 0 else 0.0
            phases.append({"K": K, "w": w, "delta": delta, "cpi": cpi})

        weighted_cpi = sum(p["w"] * p["cpi"] for p in phases)
        estimated_cycles = int(args.icount * weighted_cpi)

        print(f"M4 profile: {len(slice_indices)} BBV rows ({bbv.name})")
        print(f"cluster: {len(phases)} phases")
        for p in phases:
            print(f"phase K={p['K']} w={p['w']:.3f} "

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# RED test for F14: `./hsim estimate <elf>` with NO --icount flag should
# run the workload to its natural exit (semihosting EXIT), drain state in
# the plugin's atexit callback, and use the plugin-counted retired-insn
# count as N_total in the SimPoint aggregation formula.
#
# Workload: tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.elf. ~50K-100K insns total,
# no csrwi 0x7C0, exits via libgloss_vh semihosting EXIT.
#
# Pipeline (profile + cluster + per-phase drains) caps at 120s for headroom.

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"

fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }

[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"
ELF="$ROOT/tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.elf"
[ -f "$ELF" ] || fail "missing fixture: $ELF (run ./hsim build first)"

cd "$ROOT"

# 1. Fixture sanity: must contain zero csrwi 0x7C0,* insns.
# Locate the riscv64 objdump (mirrors fixtures/Makefile TOOLCHAIN lookup).
OBJDUMP=""
if [ -n "${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump" ]; then
  OBJDUMP="$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump"
elif command -v riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  OBJDUMP=$(command -v riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump)
fi
if [ -n "$OBJDUMP" ]; then
  count=$("$OBJDUMP" -d "$ELF" 2>/dev/null | grep -cE 'csrwi[[:space:]]+0x7[Cc]0' || true)
  [ "${count:-0}" -eq 0 ] \
    || fail "fixture $ELF has $count csrwi 0x7C0 insn(s); F14 needs zero"
  ok "fixture has zero csrwi 0x7C0 insns"
else
  echo "[skip] objdump not found; skipping csrwi check"
fi

# 2. estimate --help mentions the new --icount 0 semantics.
out=$("$HSIM" estimate --help 2>&1) || fail "estimate --help non-zero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qiE '(run to (program )?exit|0[[:space:]]*(=|means))' \
  || fail "estimate --help missing 'run to exit' / '0 means ...' hint:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimate --help documents --icount 0 (run-to-exit) semantics"

# 3. Real estimate run with NO --icount flag uses run-to-exit path.
set +e
out=$(timeout 120 "$HSIM" estimate "$ELF" --slice 1000 --max-k 2 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 0 ] || fail "estimate (no --icount) rc=$rc:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimate without --icount exits 0"

# 4. Output has estimated_cycles=<int>, > 0.
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'estimated_cycles=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing 'estimated_cycles=<int>' line:"$'\n'"$out"
EST=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'estimated_cycles=[0-9]+' | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
[ "${EST:-0}" -gt 0 ] \
  || fail "estimated_cycles must be > 0, got '$EST':"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimated_cycles=$EST"

# 5. N_total surfaced in the report line must be > 0 and >> 2000
# (loop iters lower bound; libgloss startup adds more).
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'N=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing 'N=<int>' in summary line:"$'\n'"$out"
NTOT=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'N=[0-9]+' | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
[ "${NTOT:-0}" -gt 2000 ] \
  || fail "N_total must reflect real insn count (>2000), got '$NTOT':"$'\n'"$out"
ok "N_total=$NTOT (from plugin-counted minstret, not args.icount)"

# 6. Per-phase rows show delta_mcycle > 0.
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing per-phase 'delta_mcycle=<int>' rows:"$'\n'"$out"
N_PHASES=$(echo "$out" | grep -cE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+')
[ "$N_PHASES" -ge 1 ] \
  || fail "need at least 1 per-phase row, got $N_PHASES:"$'\n'"$out"
while IFS= read -r delta; do
  [ "$delta" -gt 0 ] \
    || fail "delta_mcycle must be > 0, got '$delta':"$'\n'"$out"
done < <(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+' | cut -d= -f2)
ok "$N_PHASES per-phase row(s) with delta_mcycle > 0"

# 7. weighted_CPI is sensible (positive, < 100).
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'weighted_CPI=[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing 'weighted_CPI=<float>' in summary:"$'\n'"$out"
CPI=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'weighted_CPI=[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
awk -v c="$CPI" 'BEGIN { exit (c > 0 && c < 100) ? 0 : 1 }' \
  || fail "weighted_CPI=$CPI out of plausible range:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "weighted_CPI=$CPI in plausible range"

echo "PASS test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit"

  [ "${count:-0}" -eq 0 ] \
    || fail "fixture $ELF has $count csrwi 0x7C0 insn(s); F14 needs zero"
  ok "fixture has zero csrwi 0x7C0 insns"
else
  echo "[skip] objdump not found; skipping csrwi check"
fi

# 2. estimate --help mentions the new --icount 0 semantics.
out=$("$HSIM" estimate --help 2>&1) || fail "estimate --help non-zero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qiE '(run to (program )?exit|0[[:space:]]*(=|means))' \
  || fail "estimate --help missing 'run to exit' / '0 means ...' hint:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimate --help documents --icount 0 (run-to-exit) semantics"

# 3. Real estimate run with NO --icount flag uses run-to-exit path.
set +e
out=$(timeout 120 "$HSIM" estimate "$ELF" --slice 1000 --max-k 2 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 0 ] || fail "estimate (no --icount) rc=$rc:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimate without --icount exits 0"

# 4. Output has estimated_cycles=<int>, > 0.
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'estimated_cycles=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing 'estimated_cycles=<int>' line:"$'\n'"$out"
EST=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'estimated_cycles=[0-9]+' | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
[ "${EST:-0}" -gt 0 ] \
  || fail "estimated_cycles must be > 0, got '$EST':"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimated_cycles=$EST"

# 5. N_total surfaced in the report line must be > 0 and >> 2000
# (loop iters lower bound; libgloss startup adds more).
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'N=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing 'N=<int>' in summary line:"$'\n'"$out"
NTOT=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'N=[0-9]+' | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
[ "${NTOT:-0}" -gt 2000 ] \
  || fail "N_total must reflect real insn count (>2000), got '$NTOT':"$'\n'"$out"
ok "N_total=$NTOT (from plugin-counted minstret, not args.icount)"

# 6. Per-phase rows show delta_mcycle > 0.
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing per-phase 'delta_mcycle=<int>' rows:"$'\n'"$out"
N_PHASES=$(echo "$out" | grep -cE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+')
[ "$N_PHASES" -ge 1 ] \
  || fail "need at least 1 per-phase row, got $N_PHASES:"$'\n'"$out"
while IFS= read -r delta; do
  [ "$delta" -gt 0 ] \
    || fail "delta_mcycle must be > 0, got '$delta':"$'\n'"$out"
done < <(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+' | cut -d= -f2)
ok "$N_PHASES per-phase row(s) with delta_mcycle > 0"

# 7. weighted_CPI is sensible (positive, < 100).
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'weighted_CPI=[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing 'weighted_CPI=<float>' in summary:"$'\n'"$out"
CPI=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'weighted_CPI=[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
awk -v c="$CPI" 'BEGIN { exit (c > 0 && c < 100) ? 0 : 1 }' \
  || fail "weighted_CPI=$CPI out of plausible range:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "weighted_CPI=$CPI in plausible range"

echo "PASS test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit"

lastPrompt: review all code, test cases and documents to see if any inconsistency or legacy code left.

# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V

# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)

RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf \
                    no_trigger_exit
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
#
# Toolchain location comes from the repo-root config.env (loaded by
# `source ./setup.sh`, which exports HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN). Override on the
# command line with `make TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain ...` if you want
# to bypass the env var.

TOOLCHAIN ?= $(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)
ifeq ($(strip $(TOOLCHAIN)),)
$(error HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN is not set. Run `source ./setup.sh` from the repo root, or pass `TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain` on the command line)
endif
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T test.ld -nostdlib -static

# All fixtures share test.ld (load base 0x00100000, andes_ae350 DRAM).
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump demo

all: spin.elf icount_spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Note: handoff.elf, handoff_exit.elf, spin.elf are legacy (referenced only
# by EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL handoff_smoke.cpp). They still build for completeness
# but are not in active CTest. All targets now share test.ld at 0x00100000.

# Switching-mode demo runner: builds the fixtures if needed, then walks
# T1..T7 from $(DEMO_DIR), printing a banner per mode and a final
# pass/fail tally. Honors FILTER=<regex> to subset the run.
demo: all
	@bash demo.sh

spin.elf: spin.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

# CSR-less spin for F5 plugin-icount e2e. Linked at 0x00100000 (test.ld)
# so andes_ae350's MROM at 0x80000000 stays clear when loaded via -bios.
icount_spin.elf: icount_spin.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c icount_spin.S -o icount_spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) icount_spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x00100000) -- ld
# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime + v_regfile_xfer.c, different
# -march. Listed separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally
# drop V.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
	  $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

lines: # before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
-RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
+RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf \
+                    no_trigger_exit
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
behavioral: F14 implementation - estimate --icount 0 drains at program exit

Turns the previous RED test green.

Plugin (qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c):
- New plugin arg drain_at_exit=1 enables the mode.
- Add drain_state_to_file's third parameter minstret_override (callers
  pass 0 to keep QEMU's PMU minstret; the atexit path passes the plugin
  counter).
- on_count_tick: per-insn exec cb, increments atexit_insn_count
  (registered first in vcpu_tb_trans to keep cost a single uint64 add).
- on_atexit: drain_state_to_file(0, 0, atexit_insn_count) then _exit(200);
  C11 7.22.4.4 forbids exit() inside an atexit cb.
- drained_already guards against double-drain when a CSR/PC/icount/slice
  trigger fires before natural exit.

hsim cmd_estimate:
- Default --icount changes from 10000 to 0 (= run-to-exit).
- New helper _run_drain_at_exit_profile() invokes QEMU once with
  drain_at_exit=1 + slice= (for BBV emission), parses the resulting BBV
  for row count and reads minstret from the drained state file via
  orchestrator.abi.HybridStateV1; returns (rc, n_total).
- Explicit --icount N (positive) keeps the legacy cmd_profile path with
  n_total = N.

Tests:
- tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit.sh: now PASS
  (estimated_cycles=87365, N_total=6513 from plugin counter, weighted_CPI=13.4).
- tests/hsim/test_hsim_estimate.sh (pre-F14 with explicit --icount 10000):
  still PASS - backward compatible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.content:
310	### 2.3 QEMU plugin args
311	
312	The plugin reads `arg=value` tokens after `-plugin <path>,`. They
313	select WHICH switching mode QEMU1 runs in:
314	
315	| Plugin arg           | Effect                                                                 |
316	|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
317	| `outfile=PATH`       | REQUIRED. Drained `hybrid_state_v1` is written here.                   |
318	| `enter_pc=0xADDR`    | M2: drain when PC == ADDR. Bypasses the CSR-insn matcher.              |
319	| `icount=N`           | M3: drain on the N-th retired insn (per vcpu).                         |
320	| `bbv_out=PATH`       | M4: emit one SimPoint BBV row per `slice=N` insns into PATH.           |
321	| `slice=N`            | Insns per slice (used by M4 and M5). Must be > 0.                      |
322	| `slice_at=K`         | M5: drain at the end of the K-th slice. Requires `slice=N`.            |
323	
324	If no mode arg is given, the default is CSR-insn match (M1): the
325	plugin matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` at retire.
326	
327	Example invocation (CSR mode, just the minimal arg):
328	
329	```
330	-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=/tmp/state.bin
331	```
332	
333	Example (icount mode):
334	
335	```
336	-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=/tmp/state.bin,icount=10000
337	```
338	
339	### 2.4 vsim CLI flags
340	
341	`sim_ax45mpv_premium <elf> [flags]`. The hybrid-relevant subset:
342	
343	| Flag                          | Purpose                                                  |
344	|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|

file.content:
### 2.3 QEMU plugin args

The plugin reads `arg=value` tokens after `-plugin <path>,`. They
select WHICH switching mode QEMU1 runs in:

| Plugin arg           | Effect                                                                 |
|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `outfile=PATH`       | REQUIRED. Drained `hybrid_state_v1` is written here.                   |
| `enter_pc=0xADDR`    | M2: drain when PC == ADDR. Bypasses the CSR-insn matcher.              |
| `icount=N`           | M3: drain on the N-th retired insn (per vcpu).                         |
| `bbv_out=PATH`       | M4: emit one SimPoint BBV row per `slice=N` insns into PATH.           |
| `slice=N`            | Insns per slice (used by M4 and M5). Must be > 0.                      |
| `slice_at=K`         | M5: drain at the end of the K-th slice. Requires `slice=N`.            |

If no mode arg is given, the default is CSR-insn match (M1): the
plugin matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` at retire.

Example invocation (CSR mode, just the minimal arg):

```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# RED test for F14: `./hsim estimate <elf>` with NO --icount flag should
# run the workload to its natural exit (semihosting EXIT), drain state in
# the plugin's atexit callback, and use the plugin-counted retired-insn
# count as N_total in the SimPoint aggregation formula.
#
# Workload: tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.elf. ~50K-100K insns total,
# no csrwi 0x7C0, exits via libgloss_vh semihosting EXIT.
#
# Pipeline (profile + cluster + per-phase drains) caps at 120s for headroom.

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"

fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }

[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"
ELF="$ROOT/tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.elf"
[ -f "$ELF" ] || fail "missing fixture: $ELF (run ./hsim build first)"

cd "$ROOT"

# 1. Fixture sanity: must contain zero csrwi 0x7C0,* insns.
# Locate the riscv64 objdump (mirrors fixtures/Makefile TOOLCHAIN lookup).
OBJDUMP=""
if [ -n "${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump" ]; then
  OBJDUMP="$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump"
elif command -v riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  OBJDUMP=$(command -v riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump)
fi
if [ -n "$OBJDUMP" ]; then

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
#
# Toolchain location comes from the repo-root config.env (loaded by
# `source ./setup.sh`, which exports HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN). Override on the
# command line with `make TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain ...` if you want
# to bypass the env var.

TOOLCHAIN ?= $(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)
ifeq ($(strip $(TOOLCHAIN)),)
$(error HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN is not set. Run `source ./setup.sh` from the repo root, or pass `TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain` on the command line)
endif
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T test.ld -nostdlib -static

# All fixtures share test.ld (load base 0x00100000, andes_ae350 DRAM).
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf \
                    no_trigger_exit
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump demo

all: spin.elf icount_spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Note: handoff.elf, handoff_exit.elf, spin.elf are legacy (referenced only
# by EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL handoff_smoke.cpp). They still build for completeness
# but are not in active CTest. All targets now share test.ld at 0x00100000.

# Switching-mode demo runner: builds the fixtures if needed, then walks
# T1..T7 from $(DEMO_DIR), printing a banner per mode and a final
# pass/fail tally. Honors FILTER=<regex> to subset the run.
demo: all
	@bash demo.sh

spin.elf: spin.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

# CSR-less spin for F5 plugin-icount e2e. Linked at 0x00100000 (test.ld)
# so andes_ae350's MROM at 0x80000000 stays clear when loaded via -bios.
icount_spin.elf: icount_spin.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c icount_spin.S -o icount_spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) icount_spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x00100000) -- ld
# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime + v_regfile_xfer.c, different
# -march. Listed separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally
# drop V.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
	  $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

lines: # 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf \
-                    no_trigger_exit
+                    rt_c_no_trigger_exit
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.

# count as N_total in the SimPoint aggregation formula.
#
# Workload: tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.elf. ~50K-100K insns total,

ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"

fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }

[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"
ELF="$ROOT/tests/fixtures/no_trigger_exit.elf"
[ -f "$ELF" ] || fail "missing fixture: $ELF (run ./hsim build first)"

cd "$ROOT"

# 1. Fixture sanity: must contain zero csrwi 0x7C0,* insns.
# Locate the riscv64 objdump (mirrors fixtures/Makefile TOOLCHAIN lookup).
OBJDUMP=""
if [ -n "${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump" ]; then
  OBJDUMP="$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump"
elif command -v riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  OBJDUMP=$(command -v riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump)
fi
if [ -n "$OBJDUMP" ]; then
  count=$("$OBJDUMP" -d "$ELF" 2>/dev/null | grep -cE 'csrwi[[:space:]]+0x7[Cc]0' || true)
  [ "${count:-0}" -eq 0 ] \
    || fail "fixture $ELF has $count csrwi 0x7C0 insn(s); F14 needs zero"
  ok "fixture has zero csrwi 0x7C0 insns"
else
  echo "[skip] objdump not found; skipping csrwi check"
fi

# 2. estimate --help mentions the new --icount 0 semantics.
out=$("$HSIM" estimate --help 2>&1) || fail "estimate --help non-zero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -qiE '(run to (program )?exit|0[[:space:]]*(=|means))' \
  || fail "estimate --help missing 'run to exit' / '0 means ...' hint:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimate --help documents --icount 0 (run-to-exit) semantics"

# 3. Real estimate run with NO --icount flag uses run-to-exit path.
set +e
out=$(timeout 120 "$HSIM" estimate "$ELF" --slice 1000 --max-k 2 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -eq 0 ] || fail "estimate (no --icount) rc=$rc:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimate without --icount exits 0"

# 4. Output has estimated_cycles=<int>, > 0.
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'estimated_cycles=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing 'estimated_cycles=<int>' line:"$'\n'"$out"
EST=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'estimated_cycles=[0-9]+' | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
[ "${EST:-0}" -gt 0 ] \
  || fail "estimated_cycles must be > 0, got '$EST':"$'\n'"$out"
ok "estimated_cycles=$EST"

# 5. N_total surfaced in the report line must be > 0 and >> 2000
# (loop iters lower bound; libgloss startup adds more).
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'N=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing 'N=<int>' in summary line:"$'\n'"$out"
NTOT=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'N=[0-9]+' | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
[ "${NTOT:-0}" -gt 2000 ] \
  || fail "N_total must reflect real insn count (>2000), got '$NTOT':"$'\n'"$out"
ok "N_total=$NTOT (from plugin-counted minstret, not args.icount)"

# 6. Per-phase rows show delta_mcycle > 0.
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing per-phase 'delta_mcycle=<int>' rows:"$'\n'"$out"
N_PHASES=$(echo "$out" | grep -cE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+')
[ "$N_PHASES" -ge 1 ] \
  || fail "need at least 1 per-phase row, got $N_PHASES:"$'\n'"$out"
while IFS= read -r delta; do
  [ "$delta" -gt 0 ] \
    || fail "delta_mcycle must be > 0, got '$delta':"$'\n'"$out"
done < <(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'delta_mcycle=[0-9]+' | cut -d= -f2)
ok "$N_PHASES per-phase row(s) with delta_mcycle > 0"

# 7. weighted_CPI is sensible (positive, < 100).
echo "$out" | grep -qE 'weighted_CPI=[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' \
  || fail "missing 'weighted_CPI=<float>' in summary:"$'\n'"$out"
CPI=$(echo "$out" | grep -oE 'weighted_CPI=[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
awk -v c="$CPI" 'BEGIN { exit (c > 0 && c < 100) ? 0 : 1 }' \
  || fail "weighted_CPI=$CPI out of plausible range:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "weighted_CPI=$CPI in plausible range"

echo "PASS test_hsim_estimate_drain_at_exit"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# RED test for F14: `./hsim estimate <elf>` with NO --icount flag should
# run the workload to its natural exit (semihosting EXIT), drain state in
# the plugin's atexit callback, and use the plugin-counted retired-insn
# count as N_total in the SimPoint aggregation formula.
#
# Workload: tests/fixtures/rt_c_no_trigger_exit.elf. ~50K-100K insns total,
# no csrwi 0x7C0, exits via libgloss_vh semihosting EXIT.
#
# Pipeline (profile + cluster + per-phase drains) caps at 120s for headroom.

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
* Always update docs/USER_GUIDE.md if something changed.

## Repository Overview

Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz-level) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) share architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The system runs a three-phase round-trip: **QEMU1** (drain on CSR/PC trigger) -> **vsim** (resume + execute + drain) -> **QEMU2** (restore via gdbstub, continue).

Two Git submodules:
- `QEMU/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`)
- `verilator/` - vsim sources; `hybrid/` subdirs hold the plugin and test fixtures
- `tools/simpoint/` - SimPoint 3.2 (UCSD) clustering binary; pinned to upstream `17783e5`

Shared ABI lives at `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (not inside either submodule).

## Build Commands

**Primary path (`./hsim`)** -- the end-user driver, used in all user-facing docs:

```sh
./hsim build                       # builds qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint, fixtures
./hsim test                        # full e2e suite (defaults: MODE=both, JOBS=8)
./hsim run <elf> --mode csr|pc|icount|slice  # one round-trip
./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>
```

**Developer escape hatch** -- the underlying scripts. Use these when
working on the build system, the harness, or a single leg in isolation:

```sh
# Build everything (qemu -> vsim -> plugin -> simpoint)
source ./setup.sh

# Build individual components
./scripts/build_qemu.sh                              # QEMU (configure + make -j64)
./scripts/build_vsim.sh                              # vsim (Docker/podman, runs ./build.sh inside container)
make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU  # TCG plugin (out-of-tree)
./scripts/build_simpoint.sh                          # SimPoint clustering binary (g++ on host)

# Build RISC-V ELF test fixtures
make -C tests/fixtures              # all fixtures
make -C tests/fixtures rt_c_v_regs.elf  # single fixture

# Clean
source ./setup.sh clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all]
```

**Outputs:**
- `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium` (or other CPU variant)
- `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`
- `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`

`scripts/build_vsim.sh` is Docker-based (podman). It builds an image from `verilator/Dockerfile` (target `env`) then runs `./build.sh` inside it, mounting the repo at `/work` and `include/` as `/include:ro`.

## Running Tests

```sh
# All e2e tests in parallel (auto-detects job count from nproc + /dev/shm headroom)
bash scripts/run_e2e.sh

# Run with specific options
JOBS=4 MODE=csr bash scripts/run_e2e.sh         # 4-way parallel, CSR-mode only
JOBS=1 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh                  # sequential (debug)
FILTER=rt_c_v bash scripts/run_e2e.sh           # regex filter on fixture name
LOG_DIR=/tmp/my-logs bash scripts/run_e2e.sh    # custom log directory

# Unit tests (inside vsim build container)
# The container build runs: cmake --build build -j
# To run ctest directly after build:
#   podman run ... ./build.sh && podman exec ... ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
```

**Important:** on a high-core-count machine (128+ cores) `default_jobs()` computes `min(nproc, /dev/shm_free/256MB)` which can reach 128+. Running that many vsim Verilator processes simultaneously saturates CPU and causes timeouts. Use `JOBS=8` (or lower) as a practical cap: `JOBS=8 bash scripts/run_e2e.sh`.

`scripts/run_e2e.sh` exits 0 only when all tests pass. Logs go to a `mktemp` directory under `/tmp`; path is printed at the start of each run. Each test's full output lands in `$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log`.

## Architecture

### Three-Phase Handoff

```
QEMU1 --(state file)--> vsim --(state file + V-scratch mmap)--> QEMU2
```

1. **QEMU plugin** (`qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`) - TCG plugin. On CSR-write trigger (`csrwi 0x7C0, 0`) or PC-trigger, drains QEMU's architectural state into a binary `hybrid_state_v1` struct written to `--outfile`.
2. **vsim handoff controller** (`src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp`) - Detects the trigger inside vsim, pauses the hart via DMI, then calls `ResumeDriver` to restore state.
3. **ResumeDriver** (`src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`) - Writes GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-state into the vsim hart via RISC-V Debug Module Abstract Commands and Program Buffer.
4. **StateDrain** (`src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`) - On vsim exit trigger, reads back the full architectural state via DMI Abstract Commands.
5. **qemu_handback** (`src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`) - Spawns QEMU2, connects via gdbstub, sends P-packet register writes for GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-regs, then `continue`s.

### Trigger Modes

- **CSR mode** (default): Pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (entry) / `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (exit) at retire time. CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on Andes ax45mp.
- **PC mode**: ELF symbols `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` resolved at startup; CSR insns are bypassed.

Both modes coexist in the fixtures. `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc` selects at runtime.

### Shared ABI (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`)

`hybrid_state_v1` is the binary wire format exchanged between QEMU plugin, vsim, and the handback path. It holds: 32 GPRs, 32 FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, etc.), PMP (64 pmpaddr + 16 pmpcfg), and the V extension block (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, 32 vreg[], vxsat, vxrm, vcsr). `HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64` supports VLEN up to 512 bits.

Flags (`HYBRID_FLAG_RV64`, `HYBRID_FLAG_FP`, `HYBRID_FLAG_V`) gate which state blocks are live. The ABI struct is validated by `tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp`.

### V-State Transport

V register transport uses a fixed scratch region (`kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`) in the shared mmap. vsim's inline asm writes all 32 V regs there via `vse8.v`; `StateDrain` reads those bytes into `hybrid_state_v1::vreg[]`. `qemu_handback` ships them to QEMU2 via gdbstub P-packets by register number.

V scalar CSR transport (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr):
- **Tier A** (Abstract Commands): vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, mstatus.VS
- **Tier B** (Program Buffer): vtype + vl via `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` (direct CSR write to vtype sets vill; vl is read-only; only `vsetvl` restores both atomically)

Restore order in `ResumeDriver::resume()` when `HYBRID_FLAG_V` is set:
1. `mstatus.VS` (enables V in the hart)
2. `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in Program Buffer (stages `vl` in x5, `vtype` in x6 via abstract GPR writes first)
3. `vstart` (AFTER vsetvl: vsetvl resets vstart to 0)
4. `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`
5. GPR loop (overwrites the x5/x6 scratch - safe now)

### Test Fixtures

All fixtures run on the unified `tests/hybrid/e2e.sh` harness, which
sets up an `/dev/shm`-backed shared mmap (128 MB) so memory survives
the QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 round-trip. Two fixture families:

| Family   | Examples                                                    | Notes |
|----------|-------------------------------------------------------------|-------|
| asm      | `handoff_roundtrip`, `rt_all_gprs`, `rt_csr_survival`, `rt_shared_mem` | no C runtime; don't strictly need shared mmap but use it anyway (~20 ms cost is negligible). |
| C-level  | `rt_c_hello`, `rt_c_matmul`, `rt_c_v_regs`, `rt_c_v_csrs`   | linked against vsim-demo C runtime. Shared mmap is REQUIRED -- the stack/heap and `main()`'s ra-spill/restore around `csrwi 0x7C0,1` must survive vsim -> QEMU2. |

The shared mmap is created with `mktemp -p /dev/shm` + `truncate`,
then QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file`.

Fixture source lives in `tests/fixtures/`. C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c}` plus the Andes toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting-backed libgloss), using `rt_c.ld`. V-using fixtures require `-march=rv64gcv`. **Phase-2 rule:** the span between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()` runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (no `printf`, `fopen`, semihosting ebreak, etc.) -- vsim does not implement semihosting, so any libgloss-routed call inside the ROI traps. Phase 1 and phase 3 print via standard newlib stdio (both QEMUs are launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`).

### vsim CMake Structure

vsim lives entirely in `verilator/`. Key CMake options:
- `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID` (default OFF) - enables e2e CTest registration
- `VSIM_CONFIG_FILE` - CPU YAML (default `config.yaml`)
- `VSIM_NO_VERILATOR` - skip Verilator build (assume prebuilt)

The hybrid orchestration layer (`src/hybrid/*.hpp`) is header-only and depends only on C++20 standard library + the shared ABI header. SystemC/Verilator are not required for hybrid unit tests.

Hybrid unit tests (`tests/hybrid/`) use [doctest](https://github.com/doctest/doctest). They are registered in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` and run via `ctest`. The `recording_bus.hpp` / `RecordingSession` patterns provide duck-typed test doubles for the DMI bus and gdbstub session without requiring virtual interfaces.

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` | Wire-format struct (shared by all three components) |
| `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` | TCG plugin: drain on trigger |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` | Phase dispatcher |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` | DMI state restore (Tier A+B) |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` | DMI state capture |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` | Spawn QEMU2, gdbstub P-packets |
| `verilator/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` | V register transport via scratch region |
| `verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` | CTest registration for all e2e fixtures |
| `scripts/run_e2e.sh` | Parallel e2e runner (FIFO job pool, per-test logs) |
| `tests/fixtures/Makefile` | RISC-V ELF fixture build (Andes toolchain) |

+**Manual driver tests**: `tests/hsim/test_hsim_*.sh` are per-feature RED-then-green smoke tests for `./hsim` subcommands (F1..F14). 1	// Wire contract between the QEMU TCG plugin and vsim. Both sides write/read
2	// `struct hybrid_state_v1` byte-for-byte through a shared file.
3	//
4	// Stability: never repurpose offsets within a published version. Add new
5	// fields by consuming bytes from `reserved`, or by introducing v2 alongside.
6	//
7	// C and C++ both compile this header.
8	
9	#ifndef HYBRID_STATE_ABI_H
10	#define HYBRID_STATE_ABI_H
11	
12	#include <stdint.h>
13	#include <stddef.h>
14	
15	#ifdef __cplusplus
16	extern "C" {
17	#endif
18	
19	#define HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC   0x42594841u  // 'AHYB' little-endian
20	#define HYBRID_STATE_VERSION 1u
21	
22	#define HYBRID_FLAG_RV64 (1u << 0)
23	#define HYBRID_FLAG_FP   (1u << 1)
24	#define HYBRID_FLAG_V    (1u << 2)
25	
26	// VLEN upper bound, in bytes. Source of truth: data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg
27	// which sets `NDS_VLEN 512` (-> 64 bytes per vector register). When that
28	// configured value changes, this constant must be updated in lock-step so
29	// the wire-format struct grows or shrinks to match the configured RVV width.
30	#define HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX 64
31	
32	// 64 PMP entries; pmpcfg slots reserved at 16 so the layout works for both
33	// RV64 (8 entries/CSR) and a future RV32 (4 entries/CSR) packing.
34	#define HYBRID_PMP_CFG_NUM  16
35	#define HYBRID_PMP_ADDR_NUM 64
36	
37	// All fields are naturally 8-byte aligned, so __attribute__((packed)) is
38	// unnecessary and would force unaligned access codegen. Layout is locked by
39	// the static_asserts in state_abi_check.h.

input.command:
git add CLAUDE.md include/hybrid/state_abi.h && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
structural: document CSR/PMP restore split and wire-format endianness

Two related design assumptions that were never written down:

CLAUDE.md "Three-Phase Handoff": add a paragraph clarifying that the
plugin drains 11 M-mode CSRs + PMP (full set in hybrid_state_v1), but
ResumeDriver restores only mstatus / mtvec / mcycle / minstret to vsim.
The rest (mepc, mcause, mtval, mscratch, medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie,
satp, pmpcfg, pmpaddr) come back via QEMU2's gdbstub in qemu_handback.
This is correct as long as the vsim phase is trap-free and PMP-static;
a future workload needing traps inside the ROI would have to extend
ResumeDriver.

CLAUDE.md "Shared ABI" + include/hybrid/state_abi.h header: note that
hybrid_state_v1's multi-byte fields are little-endian (native on RV64
host + x86-64 dev box). The format is not endian-portable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

function(vsim_add_test TARGET SOURCE)
  add_executable(${TARGET} EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL ${SOURCE})
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} SYSTEM PRIVATE ${VSIM_INCLUDE_DIRS})
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}"
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/src"
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/src/utils")
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} SYSTEM PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/external/doctest/doctest")
  target_compile_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_FLAGS})
  target_compile_definitions(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_DEFS})
  target_link_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_LINK_FLAGS})
  add_test(NAME ${TARGET} COMMAND $<TARGET_FILE:${TARGET}>)
endfunction()

vsim_add_test(test_memory_region src/tests/platform/memory_region.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_optparse src/tests/utils/optparse.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_cpu_signal_registry src/tests/utils/cpu_signal_registry.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_sizeconvert src/tests/utils/sizeconvert.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_simple_memory src/tests/platform/simple_memory.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_mmap_backing src/tests/platform/mmap_backing.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_interconnect src/tests/platform/interconnect.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_duplicator src/tests/utils/duplicator.test.cpp)
vsim_add_test(test_kanata_logger_loopgate
  tests/pipeview/kanata_logger_loopgate.test.cpp)
